Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"375: class OutputGraph(OutputGraphGuardsState):\\n376:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n377:     Wrapper class to hold outputs of InstructionTranslator.  Mainly the\\n378:     generated fx.Graph.\\n379: \\n380:     OutputGraph is 1:1 with a frame being processed. Each frame is associated\\n381:     with some root InstructionTranslator. When user code calls a function,\\n382:     we construct a InliningInstructionTranslator that continues to write into\\n383:     the root InstructionTranslator's OutputGraph.\\n384:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n385: \\n386:     side_effects: SideEffects\\n387: \\n388:     def __init__(\\n389:         self,\\n390:         code_options: dict[str, Any],\\n391:         compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn],\\n392:         root_tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\",\\n393:         export: bool,\\n394:         export_constraints: Sequence[_ConstraintTarget],\\n395:         frame_state: Any,\\n396:         local_scope: Scope,\\n397:         global_scope: Scope,\\n398:         f_code: CodeType,\\n399:         torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode],\\n400:         package: Optional[\\\"CompilePackage\\\"],\\n401:     ) -> None:\\n402:         super().__init__(\\n403:             local_scope,\\n404:             global_scope,\\n405:             torch_function_mode_stack,\\n406:             guard_on_key_order=set(),\\n407:             input_source_to_sizes_strides={},\\n408:             dual_level=torch.autograd.forward_ad._current_level,\\n409:             functorch_layers=torch._functorch.pyfunctorch.retrieve_all_functorch_interpreters(),\\n410:             current_device=torch.utils._device.CURRENT_DEVICE,\\n411:             # initial_global_state is only None during NopTest.\\n412:             global_state_guard=torch._dynamo.convert_frame.initial_global_state\\n413:             or torch._C._dynamo.guards.GlobalStateGuard(),\\n414:             # These are set by @property instead, just initialize them as blank\\n415:             _guards=torch._guards.GuardsSet(),\\n416:             _aotautograd_guards=[],\\n417:         )\\n418:         self.tracers = [SubgraphTracer(self, is_export=export)]\\n419:         # Map from graph input's `Source` to its `VariableTracker` to\\n420:         # de-duplicate graph inputs by source and reuse the tracker\\n421:         self.input_source_to_var: dict[Source, VariableTracker] = {}\\n422:         self.export = export\\n423:         self.export_constraints = export_constraints  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n424:         self.frame_state = frame_state\\n425:         self.cleanup_hooks: list[Callable[[], Any]] = []\\n426:         # compile_id is an id number for the current torch.compile\\n427:         self.compile_id: int = next(_compile_id_counter)\\n428:         # Set of globals installed via install_global* APIs\\n429:         self.installed_globals: set[str] = set()\\n430: \\n431:         # TODO: maybe should just pass the entire f_code in here?  Not\\n432:         # sure...\\n433:         self.co_fields = {\\n434:             \\\"co_name\\\": f_code.co_name,\\n435:             \\\"co_filename\\\": f_code.co_filename,\\n436:             \\\"co_firstlineno\\\": f_code.co_firstlineno,\\n437:         }\\n438: \\n439:         self.region_tracker = GraphRegionTracker()\\n440: \\n441:         # tracked_fakes says where any tensor that was wrapped to fake came\\n442:         # from.  It is similar to GraphArg, in that all GraphArgs will get\\n443:         # will get added to TrackedFakes, but TrackedFakes also contains\\n444:         # GraphArgs that got pruned, and things like Tensor attributes which\\n445:         # aren't explicit graph inputs.  Used by shape guard\\n446:         self.tracked_fakes: list[TrackedFake] = []\\n447: \\n448:         shape_env = ShapeEnv(\\n449:             # Reference Cycle!\\n450:             # Share a reference to the list of TrackedFake.\\n451: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL375-450\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "375: class OutputGraph(OutputGraphGuardsState):\n376:     \"\"\"\n377:     Wrapper class to hold outputs of InstructionTranslator.  Mainly the\n378:     generated fx.Graph.\n379: \n380:     OutputGraph is 1:1 with a frame being processed. Each frame is associated\n381:     with some root InstructionTranslator. When user code calls a function,\n382:     we construct a InliningInstructionTranslator that continues to write into\n383:     the root InstructionTranslator's OutputGraph.\n384:     \"\"\"\n385: \n386:     side_effects: SideEffects\n387: \n388:     def __init__(\n389:         self,\n390:         code_options: dict[str, Any],\n391:         compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn],\n392:         root_tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\",\n393:         export: bool,\n394:         export_constraints: Sequence[_ConstraintTarget],\n395:         frame_state: Any,\n396:         local_scope: Scope,\n397:         global_scope: Scope,\n398:         f_code: CodeType,\n399:         torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode],\n400:         package: Optional[\"CompilePackage\"],\n401:     ) -> None:\n402:         super().__init__(\n403:             local_scope,\n404:             global_scope,\n405:             torch_function_mode_stack,\n406:             guard_on_key_order=set(),\n407:             input_source_to_sizes_strides={},\n408:             dual_level=torch.autograd.forward_ad._current_level,\n409:             functorch_layers=torch._functorch.pyfunctorch.retrieve_all_functorch_interpreters(),\n410:             current_device=torch.utils._device.CURRENT_DEVICE,\n411:             # initial_global_state is only None during NopTest.\n412:             global_state_guard=torch._dynamo.convert_frame.initial_global_state\n413:             or torch._C._dynamo.guards.GlobalStateGuard(),\n414:             # These are set by @property instead, just initialize them as blank\n415:             _guards=torch._guards.GuardsSet(),\n416:             _aotautograd_guards=[],\n417:         )\n418:         self.tracers = [SubgraphTracer(self, is_export=export)]\n419:         # Map from graph input's `Source` to its `VariableTracker` to\n420:         # de-duplicate graph inputs by source and reuse the tracker\n421:         self.input_source_to_var: dict[Source, VariableTracker] = {}\n422:         self.export = export\n423:         self.export_constraints = export_constraints  # type: ignore[assignment]\n424:         self.frame_state = frame_state\n425:         self.cleanup_hooks: list[Callable[[], Any]] = []\n426:         # compile_id is an id number for the current torch.compile\n427:         self.compile_id: int = next(_compile_id_counter)\n428:         # Set of globals installed via install_global* APIs\n429:         self.installed_globals: set[str] = set()\n430: \n431:         # TODO: maybe should just pass the entire f_code in here?  Not\n432:         # sure...\n433:         self.co_fields = {\n434:             \"co_name\": f_code.co_name,\n435:             \"co_filename\": f_code.co_filename,\n436:             \"co_firstlineno\": f_code.co_firstlineno,\n437:         }\n438: \n439:         self.region_tracker = GraphRegionTracker()\n440: \n441:         # tracked_fakes says where any tensor that was wrapped to fake came\n442:         # from.  It is similar to GraphArg, in that all GraphArgs will get\n443:         # will get added to TrackedFakes, but TrackedFakes also contains\n444:         # GraphArgs that got pruned, and things like Tensor attributes which\n445:         # aren't explicit graph inputs.  Used by shape guard\n446:         self.tracked_fakes: list[TrackedFake] = []\n447: \n448:         shape_env = ShapeEnv(\n449:             # Reference Cycle!\n450:             # Share a reference to the list of TrackedFake.\n451: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL375-450"

{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"800:         tracer = InstructionTranslator(\\n801:             instructions,\\n802:             code,\\n803:             locals,\\n804:             globals,\\n805:             builtins,\\n806:             closure,\\n807:             tf_mode_stack,\\n808:             code_options,\\n809:             compiler_fn,\\n810:             one_graph,\\n811:             export,\\n812:             export_constraints,\\n813:             frame_state=frame_state,\\n814:             speculation_log=speculation_log,  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n815:             exn_vt_stack=exn_vt_stack,\\n816:             distributed_state=distributed_state,  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n817:             package=package,\\n818:         )\\n819: \\n820:         try:\\n821:             tracer.output.mark_bytecode_tracing_start()\\n822:             with tracing(tracer.output.tracing_context), tracer.set_current_tx():\\n823:                 tracer.run()\\n824:         except exc.UnspecializeRestartAnalysis:\\n825:             speculation_log.clear()  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n826:             raise\\n827:         except (\\n828:             exc.SpeculationRestartAnalysis,\\n829:             exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis,\\n830:             exc.SkipFrame,\\n831:         ):\\n832:             raise\\n833:         except Exception:\\n834:             if translation_validation_enabled():\\n835:                 bisect(tracer.output.shape_env)\\n836:             raise\\n837:         finally:\\n838:             tracer.output.call_cleanup_hooks()\\n839: \\n840:         output = tracer.output\\n841:         assert output is not None\\n842:         assert output.output_instructions\\n843:         instructions[:] = output.output_instructions\\n844:         code_options.update(output.code_options)\\n845:         propagate_inst_exn_table_entries(instructions)\\n846:         check_inst_exn_tab_entries_valid(instructions)\\n847:         instructions[:] = remove_pointless_jumps(remove_dead_code(instructions))\\n848: \\n849:     @compile_time_strobelight_meta(phase_name=\\\"compile_inner\\\")\\n850:     def compile_inner(\\n851:         code: CodeType,\\n852:         one_graph: bool,\\n853:         hooks: Hooks,\\n854:         transform: Callable[[list[Instruction], dict[str, Any]], Any],\\n855:     ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\\n856:         with contextlib.ExitStack() as stack:\\n857:             stack.enter_context(\\n858:                 torch._dynamo.callback_handler.install_callbacks(\\n859:                     CallbackTrigger.DYNAMO, str(CompileContext.current_compile_id())\\n860:                 )\\n861:             )\\n862:             stack.enter_context(CompileTimeInstructionCounter.record())\\n863:             return _compile_inner(code, one_graph, hooks, transform)\\n864: \\n865:         return (\\n866:             ConvertFrameReturn()\\n867:         )  # dead, but see https://github.com/python/mypy/issues/7577\\n868: \\n869:     @maybe_cprofile\\n870:     def _compile_inner(\\n871:         code: CodeType,\\n872:         one_graph: bool,\\n873:         hooks: Hooks,\\n874:         transform: Callable[[list[Instruction], dict[str, Any]], Any],\\n875:     ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\\n876:         nonlocal dynamo_time_before_restart\\n877:         last_attempt_start_time = start_time = time.time()\\n878: \\n879:         def log_bytecode(\\n880:             prefix: str, name: str, filename: str, line_no: int, code: CodeType\\n881:         ) -> None:\\n882:             if bytecode_log.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG):\\n883:                 bytecode_log.debug(\\n884:                     format_bytecode(prefix, name, filename, line_no, code)\\n885:                 )\\n886: \\n887:         log_bytecode(\\n888:             \\\"ORIGINAL BYTECODE\\\",\\n889:             code.co_name,\\n890:             code.co_filename,\\n891:             code.co_firstlineno,\\n892:             code,\\n893:         )\\n894: \\n895:         out_code = None\\n896:         for attempt in itertools.count():\\n897:             CompileContext.get().attempt = attempt\\n898:             try:\\n899:                 with dynamo_timed(\\n900:                     f\\\"compile_attempt_{attempt}\\\", log_pt2_compile_event=True\\n901: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py%3FL800-900\"}",

  "content": "{\"blocks\":[{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"335: # fn: transforms an old FX node into one corresponding to the newly created ShapeEnv.\\n336: def replay_shape_env_events(events):\\n337:     from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import ShapeEnv\\n338: \",\"endLine\":338,\"startLine\":335},{\"content\":\"189: # also check that the ShapeEnv instance is the same for all of them.\\n190: def _extract_shape_env_and_assert_equal(args, kwargs):\\n191:     from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import is_symbolic, ShapeEnv, SymTypes\\n192: \",\"endLine\":192,\"startLine\":189},{\"content\":\"432: # returned by ShapeEnv.produce_guards.\\n433: def shape_env_check_state_equal(env1, env2, non_state_variable_names, map_value):\\n434:     # Collect and remove variables that don't necessarily represent the state\\n435: \",\"endLine\":435,\"startLine\":432}],\"file\":\"torch/fx/experimental/recording.py\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/blob/torch/fx/experimental/recording.py\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"230:             symbolic_context.constraint_sizes[i] = constraint.constraint_range\\n231:             mode.shape_env.source_name_to_debug_name[src.name()] = constraint.name  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n232:     fake = mode.from_tensor(t, source=source, symbolic_context=symbolic_context)\\n233:     mode.shape_env.tracked_fakes.append(TrackedFake(fake, source, symbolic_context))  # type: ignore[union-attr]\\n234:     return fake\\n235: \",\"endLine\":235,\"startLine\":230},{\"content\":\"491:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n492:     shape_env = fake_mode.shape_env\\n493:     assert shape_env is not None\\n494:     assert shape_env.tracked_fakes is not None\\n495: \\n496:     placeholders = [tf.fake for tf in shape_env.tracked_fakes]\\n497:     sources = [tf.source for tf in shape_env.tracked_fakes]\\n498:     input_contexts = [tf.symbolic_context for tf in shape_env.tracked_fakes]\\n499:     constraint_violation_error = None\\n500: \",\"endLine\":500,\"startLine\":491},{\"content\":\"629: \\n630:     shape_env = fake_mode.shape_env\\n631:     assert shape_env is not None\\n632:     inline_constraints = gm.meta.get(\\\"inline_constraints\\\", [])\\n633: \",\"endLine\":633,\"startLine\":629}],\"file\":\"torch/_export/non_strict_utils.py\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/blob/torch/_export/non_strict_utils.py\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"2169: @dataclass\\n2170: class TrackedFake:\\n2171:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2172: \",\"endLine\":2172,\"startLine\":2169},{\"content\":\"2141:     # get recorded in var_to_val, etc.\\n2142:     # TODO(voz): consider a weakref to the shape_env here\\n2143:     shape_env_to_source_to_symbol_cache: dict[int, dict[str, sympy.Expr]] = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n2144: \\n2145: \",\"endLine\":2145,\"startLine\":2141},{\"content\":\"1115:         _free_unbacked_symbols_with_path,\\n1116:         shape_env=shape_env,\\n1117:         pending=pending,\\n1118: \",\"endLine\":1118,\"startLine\":1115}],\"file\":\"torch/fx/experimental/symbolic_shapes.py\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/blob/torch/fx/experimental/symbolic_shapes.py\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"329:     @property\\n330:     def shape_env(self) -> ShapeEnv:\\n331:         raise AssertionError(f\\\"shape_env shouldn't be accessed from {type(self)}\\\")\\n332: \\n333: \",\"endLine\":333,\"startLine\":329},{\"content\":\"828:     @property\\n829:     def shape_env(self) -> ShapeEnv:\\n830:         assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode is not None\\n831:         assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode.shape_env is not None\\n832:         return self.tracing_context.fake_mode.shape_env\\n833: \\n834: \",\"endLine\":834,\"startLine\":828},{\"content\":\"469:             fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(\\n470:                 shape_env=shape_env,\\n471:                 # TODO (tmanlaibaatar) Remove this once we always lift params and buffers\\n472: \",\"endLine\":472,\"startLine\":469}],\"file\":\"torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"2317: \\n2318:         shape_env = self.tx.output.shape_env\\n2319:         if TracingContext.get().force_unspec_int_unbacked_size_like:\\n2320:             wrapped_value = shape_env.create_unbacked_symint()\\n2321:             _constrain_range_for_size(wrapped_value)\\n2322: \",\"endLine\":2322,\"startLine\":2317},{\"content\":\"70:     SymIntSymbolicContext,\\n71:     TrackedFake,\\n72: )\\n73: \",\"endLine\":73,\"startLine\":70},{\"content\":\"3457:         tensor_source=source,\\n3458:         shape_env_to_source_to_symbol_cache=shape_env_to_source_to_symbol_cache,\\n3459:     )\\n3460: \",\"endLine\":3460,\"startLine\":3457}],\"file\":\"torch/_dynamo/variables/builder.py\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/variables/builder.py\"}]}",

  "content": "{\"content\":\"2169: @dataclass\\n2170: class TrackedFake:\\n2171:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2172:     Tracks the sources of all fake tensors we wrap in Dynamo.\\n2173:     Used by shape guard computation.\\n2174:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2175: \\n2176:     fake: Union[FakeTensor, SymInt]\\n2177:     source: Source\\n2178:     symbolic_context: Optional[SymbolicContext]\\n2179: \\n2180:     def __hash__(self) -> int:\\n2181:         return hash((self.fake, self.source.name()))\\n2182: \\n2183:     def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool:\\n2184:         if isinstance(other, TrackedFake):\\n2185:             return self.fake is other.fake and self.source.name() == other.source.name()\\n2186: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/fx/experimental/symbolic_shapes.py%3FL2169-2185\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "2169: @dataclass\n2170: class TrackedFake:\n2171:     \"\"\"\n2172:     Tracks the sources of all fake tensors we wrap in Dynamo.\n2173:     Used by shape guard computation.\n2174:     \"\"\"\n2175: \n2176:     fake: Union[FakeTensor, SymInt]\n2177:     source: Source\n2178:     symbolic_context: Optional[SymbolicContext]\n2179: \n2180:     def __hash__(self) -> int:\n2181:         return hash((self.fake, self.source.name()))\n2182: \n2183:     def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool:\n2184:         if isinstance(other, TrackedFake):\n2185:             return self.fake is other.fake and self.source.name() == other.source.name()\n2186: ",

  "content": "{\"content\":\"3565: class ShapeEnv:\\n3566:     # This is a wrapper over the actual __init__ function.\\n3567:     #\\n3568:     # Where to add a new constructor parameter to ShapeEnv?\\n3569:     # =====================================================\\n3570:     # This __init__ function should be used only for parameters related to event recording.\\n3571:     # These are parameters that we don't wish to pass down the road to new ShapeEnv instances\\n3572:     # created from replaying events.\\n3573:     #\\n3574:     # If you wish to add a parameter to the constructor of ShapeEnv, unrelated to event\\n3575:     # recording, do so in the _init function.\\n3576:     def __init__(\\n3577:         self,\\n3578:         *,\\n3579:         should_record_events: Optional[bool] = None,\\n3580:         tracked_fakes: Optional[list[Any]] = None,\\n3581:         **kwargs: Any,\\n3582:     ) -> None:\\n3583:         self._init(**kwargs)\\n3584: \\n3585:         # Disable event recording when replaying.\\n3586:         kwargs[\\\"should_record_events\\\"] = False\\n3587: \\n3588:         from torch.fx.experimental.validator import translation_validation_enabled\\n3589: \\n3590:         self._translation_validation_enabled = translation_validation_enabled()\\n3591: \\n3592:         # If not specified, enable event recording if both:\\n3593:         #   - Translation validation is on\\n3594:         #   - Translation validation bisection is not disabled\\n3595:         self.should_record_events = (\\n3596:             should_record_events\\n3597:             if should_record_events is not None\\n3598:             else (\\n3599:                 self._translation_validation_enabled\\n3600:                 and not config.translation_validation_no_bisect\\n3601:             )\\n3602:         )\\n3603: \\n3604:         # Enable event recording check if both:\\n3605:         #   - It should record events\\n3606:         #   - The recording check is enabled\\n3607:         self.check_recorded_events = (\\n3608:             self.should_record_events and config.check_shape_env_recorded_events\\n3609:         )\\n3610: \\n3611:         # This will make sure we only record the top-level function call.\\n3612:         self.is_recording = False\\n3613:         # Keep track of the list of tracked fakes.\\n3614:         self.tracked_fakes = tracked_fakes\\n3615:         # List of events for reconstructing ShapeEnv at arbitrary points in time.\\n3616:         self.events: list[ShapeEnvEvent] = (\\n3617:             [ShapeEnvEvent(ShapeEnv, kwargs=kwargs)]\\n3618:             if self.should_record_events\\n3619:             else []\\n3620:         )\\n3621: \\n3622:         # FakeTensor per-ShapeEnv operation cache. This is used for caching\\n3623:         # operations that contain symbolic shapes which have guards on the\\n3624:         # ShapeEnv (so are ShapeEnv-dependent).\\n3625:         #\\n3626:         # NOTE: It's important that SymNodes in this cache have their ShapeEnv\\n3627:         # stripped otherwise you end up with cycles which can only be cleaned\\n3628:         # with the GC.\\n3629:         self.fake_tensor_cache: dict[\\n3630:             torch._subclasses.fake_tensor._DispatchCacheKey,\\n3631:             torch._subclasses.fake_tensor._DispatchCacheEntry,\\n3632:         ] = {}\\n3633: \\n3634:     # Pro-tip: if you add new field to ShapeEnv, this affects some accept\\n3635:     # tests.  Accept their output with:\\n3636:     #\\n3637:     #   EXPECTTEST_ACCEPT=1 python test/dynamo/test_dynamic_shapes.py -k test_shape_env_equal\\n3638:     #\\n3639:     def _init(\\n3640:         self,\\n3641:         *,\\n3642:         allow_scalar_outputs: bool = True,\\n3643:         allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops: bool = True,\\n3644:         # NB: These are legacy configuration that help us make good choices\\n3645:         # when the constraint/dynamic dims are not explicitly passed to us.\\n3646:         # Ideally we will fix all call sites to be explicit and not have\\n3647:         # implicit choices, but this apparently was pretty involved.\\n3648:         assume_static_by_default: bool = False,\\n3649:         # Note - On 0/1 specialization\\n3650:         #\\n3651:         # The following options affect decisions we make about eager\\n3652:         # specialization.  Disabling them will increase trace time (as we do\\n3653:         # more symbolic reasoning) and can also harm the quality of generated\\n3654:         # code (because inductor may not be able to specialize for bounds\\n3655:         # being equal--although if we later respecialize because of a guard,\\n3656:         # your code may be just as good as it was before.)\\n3657:         #\\n3658:         # When True, eagerly specialize input sizes which have 0/1.\\n3659:         specialize_zero_one: bool = True,\\n3660:         # When True, assume input sizes which have the same size are\\n3661:         # symbolically equal.\\n3662:         duck_shape: Optional[bool] = None,\\n3663:         # For debugging\\n3664:         co_fields: Optional[dict[str, str]] = None,\\n3665:         # When True, whenever safe, we will generate a deferred runtime assert\\n3666:         # instead of a guard whenever we know that an expression must be True,\\n3667:         # otherwise it would be an error, even for backed SymInts (where we\\n3668:         # could ostensibly unconditionally generate guards).  This is useful\\n3669:         # for export, where preventing \\\"error checking\\\" sizes from showing up\\n3670:         # in guards is helpful, since these guards in some sense are overly\\n3671:         # pedantic.  See also https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/121749\\n3672:         prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards: bool = False,\\n3673:         # When True, does not emit or raise constraint violation errors on\\n3674:         # implicit guards generated by ops, and defers to runtime assertions\\n3675:         # in the graph instead. For export.\\n3676:         allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts: bool = False,\\n3677:         # XXX Add any new settings that could affect FakeTensor evaluation\\n3678:         # to: torch._subclasses.fake_tensor._ShapeEnvSettings\\n3679:         trace_asserts: bool = False,\\n3680:     ) -> None:\\n3681:         if duck_shape is None:\\n3682:             duck_shape = config.use_duck_shape\\n3683: \\n3684:         self.settings = ShapeEnvSettings(\\n3685:             # Not directly used by ShapeEnv; indirectly used by FakeTensor\\n3686:             allow_scalar_outputs=allow_scalar_outputs,\\n3687:             allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops,\\n3688:             # End\\n3689:             assume_static_by_default=assume_static_by_default,\\n3690:             specialize_zero_one=specialize_zero_one,\\n3691:             duck_shape=duck_shape,\\n3692:             prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards=prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards,\\n3693:             allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts=allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts,\\n3694:             trace_asserts=trace_asserts,\\n3695:         )\\n3696: \\n3697:         self.guards: list[ShapeGuard] = []\\n3698:         self.axioms: dict[sympy.Expr, sympy.Expr] = {}\\n3699: \\n3700:         # A set of ids that have already been allocated. This is used\\n3701: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/fx/experimental/symbolic_shapes.py%3FL3565-3700\"}",

    "content": "3565: class ShapeEnv:\n3566:     # This is a wrapper over the actual __init__ function.\n3567:     #\n3568:     # Where to add a new constructor parameter to ShapeEnv?\n3569:     # =====================================================\n3570:     # This __init__ function should be used only for parameters related to event recording.\n3571:     # These are parameters that we don't wish to pass down the road to new ShapeEnv instances\n3572:     # created from replaying events.\n3573:     #\n3574:     # If you wish to add a parameter to the constructor of ShapeEnv, unrelated to event\n3575:     # recording, do so in the _init function.\n3576:     def __init__(\n3577:         self,\n3578:         *,\n3579:         should_record_events: Optional[bool] = None,\n3580:         tracked_fakes: Optional[list[Any]] = None,\n3581:         **kwargs: Any,\n3582:     ) -> None:\n3583:         self._init(**kwargs)\n3584: \n3585:         # Disable event recording when replaying.\n3586:         kwargs[\"should_record_events\"] = False\n3587: \n3588:         from torch.fx.experimental.validator import translation_validation_enabled\n3589: \n3590:         self._translation_validation_enabled = translation_validation_enabled()\n3591: \n3592:         # If not specified, enable event recording if both:\n3593:         #   - Translation validation is on\n3594:         #   - Translation validation bisection is not disabled\n3595:         self.should_record_events = (\n3596:             should_record_events\n3597:             if should_record_events is not None\n3598:             else (\n3599:                 self._translation_validation_enabled\n3600:                 and not config.translation_validation_no_bisect\n3601:             )\n3602:         )\n3603: \n3604:         # Enable event recording check if both:\n3605:         #   - It should record events\n3606:         #   - The recording check is enabled\n3607:         self.check_recorded_events = (\n3608:             self.should_record_events and config.check_shape_env_recorded_events\n3609:         )\n3610: \n3611:         # This will make sure we only record the top-level function call.\n3612:         self.is_recording = False\n3613:         # Keep track of the list of tracked fakes.\n3614:         self.tracked_fakes = tracked_fakes\n3615:         # List of events for reconstructing ShapeEnv at arbitrary points in time.\n3616:         self.events: list[ShapeEnvEvent] = (\n3617:             [ShapeEnvEvent(ShapeEnv, kwargs=kwargs)]\n3618:             if self.should_record_events\n3619:             else []\n3620:         )\n3621: \n3622:         # FakeTensor per-ShapeEnv operation cache. This is used for caching\n3623:         # operations that contain symbolic shapes which have guards on the\n3624:         # ShapeEnv (so are ShapeEnv-dependent).\n3625:         #\n3626:         # NOTE: It's important that SymNodes in this cache have their ShapeEnv\n3627:         # stripped otherwise you end up with cycles which can only be cleaned\n3628:         # with the GC.\n3629:         self.fake_tensor_cache: dict[\n3630:             torch._subclasses.fake_tensor._DispatchCacheKey,\n3631:             torch._subclasses.fake_tensor._DispatchCacheEntry,\n3632:         ] = {}\n3633: \n3634:     # Pro-tip: if you add new field to ShapeEnv, this affects some accept\n3635:     # tests.  Accept their output with:\n3636:     #\n3637:     #   EXPECTTEST_ACCEPT=1 python test/dynamo/test_dynamic_shapes.py -k test_shape_env_equal\n3638:     #\n3639:     def _init(\n3640:         self,\n3641:         *,\n3642:         allow_scalar_outputs: bool = True,\n3643:         allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops: bool = True,\n3644:         # NB: These are legacy configuration that help us make good choices\n3645:         # when the constraint/dynamic dims are not explicitly passed to us.\n3646:         # Ideally we will fix all call sites to be explicit and not have\n3647:         # implicit choices, but this apparently was pretty involved.\n3648:         assume_static_by_default: bool = False,\n3649:         # Note - On 0/1 specialization\n3650:         #\n3651:         # The following options affect decisions we make about eager\n3652:         # specialization.  Disabling them will increase trace time (as we do\n3653:         # more symbolic reasoning) and can also harm the quality of generated\n3654:         # code (because inductor may not be able to specialize for bounds\n3655:         # being equal--although if we later respecialize because of a guard,\n3656:         # your code may be just as good as it was before.)\n3657:         #\n3658:         # When True, eagerly specialize input sizes which have 0/1.\n3659:         specialize_zero_one: bool = True,\n3660:         # When True, assume input sizes which have the same size are\n3661:         # symbolically equal.\n3662:         duck_shape: Optional[bool] = None,\n3663:         # For debugging\n3664:         co_fields: Optional[dict[str, str]] = None,\n3665:         # When True, whenever safe, we will generate a deferred runtime assert\n3666:         # instead of a guard whenever we know that an expression must be True,\n3667:         # otherwise it would be an error, even for backed SymInts (where we\n3668:         # could ostensibly unconditionally generate guards).  This is useful\n3669:         # for export, where preventing \"error checking\" sizes from showing up\n3670:         # in guards is helpful, since these guards in some sense are overly\n3671:         # pedantic.  See also https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/121749\n3672:         prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards: bool = False,\n3673:         # When True, does not emit or raise constraint violation errors on\n3674:         # implicit guards generated by ops, and defers to runtime assertions\n3675:         # in the graph instead. Used by shape guard\\n446:         self.tracked_fakes: list[TrackedFake] = []\\n447: \\n448:         shape_env = ShapeEnv(\\n449:             # Reference Cycle!\\n450:             # Share a reference to the list of TrackedFake.\\n451:             #\\n452:             # ShapeEnv needs this in order to be able to reproduce the call\\n453:             # to produce_guards at an arbitrary time point. That is because\\n454:             # TrackedFake instances may have its metadata changed throughout\\n455:             # the program execution.\\n456:             tracked_fakes=self.tracked_fakes,\\n457:             allow_scalar_outputs=config.capture_scalar_outputs,\\n458:             allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=config.capture_dynamic_output_shape_ops,\\n459:             prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards=config.prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards,\\n460:             allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts=config.allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts,\\n461:             co_fields=self.co_fields,\\n462:         )\\n463: \\n464:         # In export mode, we force the shape_env to strictly disallow any constraining\\n465:         # of the user marked dynamic dims\\n466:         import torch._functorch.config as _config\\n467: \\n468:         with _config.patch(fake_tensor_allow_unsafe_data_ptr_access=False):\\n469:             fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(\\n470:                 shape_env=shape_env,\\n471:                 # TODO (tmanlaibaatar) Remove this once we always lift params and buffers\\n472:                 allow_non_fake_inputs=True if self.export else False,\\n473:                 export=self.export,\\n474:             )\\n475:         self.tracing_context: TracingContext = TracingContext(fake_mode)\\n476:         self.tracing_context.traced_code.append(f_code)\\n477:         self.dynamo_compile_id: Optional[CompileId] = (\\n478:             CompileContext.current_compile_id()\\n479:         )\\n480:         self.init_ambient_guards()\\n481: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL445-480\"}",
  "structuredContent": {

That is because\n454:             # TrackedFake instances may have its metadata changed throughout\n455:             # the program execution.\n456:             tracked_fakes=self.tracked_fakes,\n457:             allow_scalar_outputs=config.capture_scalar_outputs,\n458:             allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=config.capture_dynamic_output_shape_ops,\n459:             prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards=config.prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards,\n460:             allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts=config.allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts,\n461:             co_fields=self.co_fields,\n462:         )\n463: \n464:         # In export mode, we force the shape_env to strictly disallow any constraining\n465:         # of the user marked dynamic dims\n466:         import torch._functorch.config as _config\n467: \n468:         with _config.patch(fake_tensor_allow_unsafe_data_ptr_access=False):\n469:             fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(\n470:                 shape_env=shape_env,\n471:                 # TODO (tmanlaibaatar) Remove this once we always lift params and buffers\n472:                 allow_non_fake_inputs=True if self.export else False,\n473:                 export=self.export,\n474:             )\n475:         self.tracing_context: TracingContext = TracingContext(fake_mode)\n476:         self.tracing_context.traced_code.append(f_code)\n477:         self.dynamo_compile_id: Optional[CompileId] = (\n478:             CompileContext.current_compile_id()\n479:         )\n480:         self.init_ambient_guards()\n481: ",

Each part should\\n1391:         have the similar amount of nodes, excluding non essential ops, defined in\\n1392:         `_EXCLUDED_NODE_KINDS`, such as `prim::Constant`.\\n1393:         If the graph has an odd number of nodes, the upper part will have one more node.\\n1394:         If the graph does not have any node that can be partitioned, return -1.\\n1395: \\n1396: \",\"endLine\":1396,\"startLine\":1386},{\"content\":\"1449: \\n1450:         return graph\\n1451: \\n1452:     def _partition_lower_graph(self) -> torch.Graph:\\n1453:         pivot = self._graph_partition_pivot()\\n1454:         if pivot == -1:\\n1455:             return torch.Graph()\\n1456:         graph = self.graph.copy()  # Copy to not mutate parent graph.\\n1457:         original_outputs = list(graph.outputs())\\n1458:         original_inputs = list(graph.inputs())\\n1459: \\n1460:         def _process_bridge_value_for_lower(\\n1461:             graph: torch.Graph, bridge_value: torch.Value\\n1462:         ) -> torch.Value:\\n1463:             # Add bridge values as lower graph inputs.\\n1464:             new_input = graph.addInput()\\n1465:             bridge_value.replaceAllUsesWith(new_input)\\n1466: \",\"endLine\":1466,\"startLine\":1449}],\"file\":\"torch/onnx/verification.py\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/blob/torch/onnx/verification.py\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"1318:     @dist_init\\n1319:     def test_graph_for_py_nested_call_itself(self):\\n1320:         self._test_graph_for_py_nested_call_itself(ExecMode.RPC_SYNC, False)\\n1321: \\n1322: \",\"endLine\":1322,\"startLine\":1318},{\"content\":\"1322:     @dist_init\\n1323:     def test_graph_for_py_nested_remote_call_itself(self):\\n1324:         self._test_graph_for_py_nested_call_itself(ExecMode.REMOTE, False)\\n1325: \\n1326: \",\"endLine\":1326,\"startLine\":1322},{\"content\":\"1028:     @dist_init\\n1029:     def test_graph_for_py_nested_call_itself_sparse(self):\\n1030:         self._test_graph_for_py_nested_call_itself(ExecMode.RPC_SYNC, True)\\n1031: \\n1032: \",\"endLine\":1032,\"startLine\":1028}],\"file\":\"torch/testing/_internal/distributed/rpc/dist_autograd_test.py\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/blob/torch/testing/_internal/distributed/rpc/dist_autograd_test.py\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"310: \\n311: def graph(model, args, verbose=False, use_strict_trace=True):\\n312:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n313:     Process a PyTorch model and produces a `GraphDef` proto that can be logged to TensorBoard.\\n314: \\n315: \",\"endLine\":315,\"startLine\":310},{\"content\":\"214: \\n215:     def to_proto(self):\\n216:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Convert graph representation of GraphPy object to TensorBoard required format.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n217:         #     @abc.abstractmethod\\n223:     def _run(self, *args, **kwargs) -> torch.fx.GraphModule: ...\\n224: \\n225:     def run(self, *args, **kwargs) -> torch.fx.GraphModule:\\n226:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Run the transform on `self.module`.\\n227: \\n228:         Note that this method may or may not mutate `self.module`, and the returned\\n229:         `GraphModule` could be either             "content": "524: \n525: class _GraphPickleData:\n526:     def __init__(self, graph: torch.fx.Graph, options: Options) -> None:\n527:         self.tracer_cls = graph._tracer_cls\n528:         self.tracer_extras = graph._tracer_extras\n529: \n530: ",
            "endLine": 530,
            "startLine": 524
          },
          {
            "content": "342: \n343:     def __init__(self, gm: torch.fx.GraphModule, options: Options) -> None:\n344:         # Need to do this to ensure the code is created for later pickling.\n345:         if isinstance(gm, torch.fx._lazy_graph_module._LazyGraphModule):\n346:             _python_code = gm._real_recompile()\n347: ",
            "endLine": 347,
            "startLine": 342
          },
          {
            "content": "348:             _python_code = gm.recompile()\n349:         self.gm_dict = gm.__dict__.copy()\n350:         del self.gm_dict[\"_graph\"]\n351:         self.graph = _GraphPickleData(gm._graph, options)\n352: \n353:     def unpickle(self, unpickle_state: _UnpickleState) -> torch.fx.GraphModule:\n354:         gm = torch.fx.GraphModule.__new__(torch.fx.GraphModule)\n355:         gm.__dict__ = self.gm_dict\n356:         gm._graph = self.graph.unpickle(gm, unpickle_state)\n357:         return gm\n358: ",
            "endLine": 358,
            "startLine": 348

            "content": "715: \n716:     def extend_acc_subgraph(self, tag: str):\n717:         \"\"\"\n718:         Extend the acc subgraph with `tag` going the reversed topological direction.\n719:         \"\"\"\n720: ",

Each part should\n1391:         have the similar amount of nodes, excluding non essential ops, defined in\n1392:         `_EXCLUDED_NODE_KINDS`, such as `prim::Constant`.\n1393:         If the graph has an odd number of nodes, the upper part will have one more node.\n1394:         If the graph does not have any node that can be partitioned, return -1.\n1395: \n1396: ",
            "endLine": 1396,
            "startLine": 1386
          },
          {
            "content": "1449: \n1450:         return graph\n1451: \n1452:     def _partition_lower_graph(self) -> torch.Graph:\n1453:         pivot = self._graph_partition_pivot()\n1454:         if pivot == -1:\n1455:             return torch.Graph()\n1456:         graph = self.graph.copy()  # Copy to not mutate parent graph.\n1457:         original_outputs = list(graph.outputs())\n1458:         original_inputs = list(graph.inputs())\n1459: \n1460:         def _process_bridge_value_for_lower(\n1461:             graph: torch.Graph, bridge_value: torch.Value\n1462:         ) -> torch.Value:\n1463:             # Add bridge values as lower graph inputs.\n1464:             new_input = graph.addInput()\n1465:             bridge_value.replaceAllUsesWith(new_input)\n1466: ",
            "endLine": 1466,
            "startLine": 1449
          }

{
  "endLine": 850,

  "startLine": 780

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"780:     # - self.current_tracer.create_graph_input\\n781:     # - self.root_tracer.create_graph_input\\n782:     # See NOTE [HigherOrderOperator tracing design] for more context.\\n783: \\n784:     def create_proxy(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> torch.fx.Proxy:\\n785:         return self.current_tracer.create_proxy(*args, **kwargs)\\n786: \\n787:     def create_node(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> torch.fx.Node:\\n788:         return self.current_tracer.create_node(*args, **kwargs)\\n789: \\n790:     def remove_node(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:\\n791:         return self.current_tracer.remove_node(*args, **kwargs)\\n792: \\n793:     @contextlib.contextmanager\\n794:     def subtracer(\\n795:         self, source_target: shape_env(self) -> ShapeEnv:\\n830:         assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode is not None\\n831:         assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode.shape_env is not None\\n832:         return {

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"2368: \\n2369: class SubgraphTracer(fx.Tracer):\\n2370:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2371:     Holds an FX graph that is being traced. OutputGraph owns a SubgraphTracer\\n2372:     and the separation of responsibilities is that SubgraphTracer is\\n2373:     responsible for building the graph while OutputGraph is responsible for\\n2374:     compiling and executing the graph.\\n2375:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2376: \\n2377:     def __init__(\\n2378:         self,\\n2379:         output_graph: \\\"OutputGraph\\\",\\n2380:         parent: Optional[\\\"SubgraphTracer\\\"] = None,\\n2381:         is_export: bool = False,\\n2382:         source_target: Optional[Target] = None,\\n2383:     ) -> None:\\n2384:         super().__init__()\\n2385:         self.output_graph = weakref.proxy(output_graph)\\n2386:         self.graph = torch.fx.Graph()\\n2387: \\n2388:         # See note [Export inputs must be explicitly passed in]\\n2389:         self.is_export = is_export\\n2390:         # Map from graph input name to its placeholder proxy object, where the\\n2391:         # map's keys give all current placeholder node names and can be used to\\n2392:         # create unique node names\\n2393:         self.input_name_to_proxy: dict[str, fx.Proxy] = {}\\n2394:         # Node => computed real value (see utils.get_real_value)\\n2395:         self.real_value_cache: dict[fx.Node, torch.Tensor] = {}\\n2396: \\n2397:         # SubgraphTracers can be nested. See NOTE [HigherOrderOperator tracing design]\\n2398:         self.parent = parent\\n2399:         self.source_target = source_target\\n2400:         # A dict mapping previously free variables (Proxy objects)\\n2401:         # to new Proxy objects that wrap inputs to this subgraph.\\n2402:         #\\n2403:         # This dict maps proxies in outer graphs to placeholders in current graph.\\n2404:         # It serves two purposes:\\n2405:         # - Proxies are associated with VariableTrackers. If we see\\n2406:         # the same VariableTracker twice (and it is a free variable),\\n2407:         # then we want to use the same Proxy in the current subgraph to\\n2408:         # record the tracing.\\n2409:         # - If we are tracing a HigherOrderOperator's body_fn, then we\\n2410:         # need to keep track of what free variables were lifted so we can\\n2411:         # rewrite the HigherOrderOperator call using the traced body_fn.\\n2412:         # Dicts maintain the order of args for the HigherOrderOperator call.\\n2413:         self.lifted_freevars: dict[fx.Proxy, fx.Proxy] = {}\\n2414: \\n2415:         # map basic symbols (unbacked and unbacked) to their bound proxies.\\n2416:         # There are only two cases where bound_symbols will be recorded:\\n2417:         # 1. when we create_graph_input for a backed SymInt that's basic symbol\\n2418:         # 2. when we track_unbacked_symbols for intermediate results that contain unbacked symints.\\n2419:         self.bound_symbols: dict[sympy.Symbol, Union[torch.fx.Proxy, LazyProxy]] = {}\\n2420: \\n2421:         self.prev_inst = None\\n2422:         # True if this tracer is currently tracing into torch.utils.checkpoint\\n2423:         # as part of speculate_subgraph.\\n2424:         self.under_activation_checkpoint = False\\n2425:         # True if we want to allow externally visible side-effects (doesn't throw error on their existence)\\n2426:         # during this tracer's tracing of torch.utils.checkpoint (via speculate_subgraph).\\n2427:         # Only safe if we know for sure that *NOT* replaying these side-effects during\\n2428:         # backward recomputation of the checkpoint region doesn't affect its correctness.\\n2429:         self.allow_side_effects_under_checkpoint = False\\n2430:         # True if we want to allow externally visible side-effects (doesn't throw error on their existence)\\n2431:         # during this tracer's tracing. This is currently only used by experimental AC out-of-tree\\n2432:         # via torch._dynamo.utils._disable_side_effect_safety_checks_for_current_subtracer.\\n2433:         # Note: Externally visible side-effects are allowed if this flag OR the above flag is True.\\n2434:         self.unsafe_allow_externally_visible_side_effects = False\\n2435: \\n2436:         # True if this tracer is currently tracing (reconstructing) into a Python generator\\n2437:         self.is_reconstructing_generator = False\\n2438: \\n2439:         self.debug_level: int = parent.debug_level + 1 if parent is not None else 0\\n2440: \\n2441:         self._cur_code = None\\n2442:         self._orig_gm_meta: Optional[list[Any]] = None\\n2443:         self._orig_gm_lineno_map: Optional[dict[int, Optional[int]]] = None\\n2444:         self._orig_gm_firstlineno: Optional[int] = None\\n2445:         # Each SubgraphTracer is associated with a source target, which indicates\\n2446:         # which operator this subgraph is attached to. We compute a source_fn_stack\\n2447:         # based on the source target. For the root tracer, it's set to [].\\n2448:         # This is useful for debugging and transforming the exported graph.\\n2449:         if self.parent is None:\\n2450:             self.source_fn_stack: list[Any] = []\\n2451: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2368-2450\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "2368: \n2369: class SubgraphTracer(fx.Tracer):\n2370:     \"\"\"\n2371:     Holds an FX graph that is being traced. OutputGraph owns a SubgraphTracer\n2372:     and the separation of responsibilities is that SubgraphTracer is\n2373:     responsible for building the graph while OutputGraph is responsible for\n2374:     compiling and executing the graph.\n2375:     \"\"\"\n2376: \n2377:     def __init__(\n2378:         self,\n2379:         output_graph: \"OutputGraph\",\n2380:         parent: Optional[\"SubgraphTracer\"] = None,\n2381:         is_export: bool = False,\n2382:         source_target: Optional[Target] = None,\n2383:     ) -> None:\n2384:         super().__init__()\n2385:         self.output_graph = weakref.proxy(output_graph)\n2386:         self.graph = torch.fx.Graph()\n2387: \n2388:         # See note [Export inputs must be explicitly passed in]\n2389:         self.is_export = is_export\n2390:         # Map from graph input name to its placeholder proxy object, where the\n2391:         # map's keys give all current placeholder node names and can be used to\n2392:         # create unique node names\n2393:         self.input_name_to_proxy: dict[str, fx.Proxy] = {}\n2394:         # Node => computed real value (see utils.get_real_value)\n2395:         self.real_value_cache: dict[fx.Node, torch.Tensor] = {}\n2396: \n2397:         # SubgraphTracers can be nested. See NOTE [HigherOrderOperator tracing design]\n2398:         self.parent = parent\n2399:         self.source_target = source_target\n2400:         # A dict mapping previously free variables (Proxy objects)\n2401:         # to new Proxy objects that wrap inputs to this subgraph.\n2402:         #\n2403:         # This dict maps proxies in outer graphs to placeholders in current graph.\n2404:         # It serves two purposes:\n2405:         # - Proxies are associated with VariableTrackers. If we see\n2406:         # the same VariableTracker twice (and it is a free variable),\n2407:         # then we want to use the same Proxy in the current subgraph to\n2408:         # record the tracing.\n2409:         # - If we are tracing a HigherOrderOperator's body_fn, then we\n2410:         # need to keep track of what free variables were lifted so we can\n2411:         # rewrite the HigherOrderOperator call using the traced body_fn.\n2412:         # Dicts maintain the order of args for the HigherOrderOperator call.\n2413:         self.lifted_freevars: dict[fx.Proxy, fx.Proxy] = {}\n2414: \n2415:         # map basic symbols (unbacked and unbacked) to their bound proxies.\n2416:         # There are only two cases where bound_symbols will be recorded:\n2417:         # 1. when we create_graph_input for a backed SymInt that's basic symbol\n2418:         # 2. when we track_unbacked_symbols for intermediate results that contain unbacked symints.\n2419:         self.bound_symbols: dict[sympy.Symbol, Union[torch.fx.Proxy, LazyProxy]] = {}\n2420: \n2421:         self.prev_inst = None\n2422:         # True if this tracer is currently tracing into torch.utils.checkpoint\n2423:         # as part of speculate_subgraph.\n2424:         self.under_activation_checkpoint = False\n2425:         # True if we want to allow externally visible side-effects (doesn't throw error on their existence)\n2426:         # during this tracer's tracing of torch.utils.checkpoint (via speculate_subgraph).\n2427:         # Only safe if we know for sure that *NOT* replaying these side-effects during\n2428:         # backward recomputation of the checkpoint region doesn't affect its correctness.\n2429:         self.allow_side_effects_under_checkpoint = False\n2430:         # True if we want to allow externally visible side-effects (doesn't throw error on their existence)\n2431:         # during this tracer's tracing. This is currently only used by experimental AC out-of-tree\n2432:         # via torch._dynamo.utils._disable_side_effect_safety_checks_for_current_subtracer.\n2433:         # Note: Externally visible side-effects are allowed if this flag OR the above flag is True.\n2434:         self.unsafe_allow_externally_visible_side_effects = False\n2435: \n2436:         # True if this tracer is currently tracing (reconstructing) into a Python generator\n2437:         self.is_reconstructing_generator = False\n2438: \n2439:         self.debug_level: int = parent.debug_level + 1 if parent is not None else 0\n2440: \n2441:         self._cur_code = None\n2442:         self._orig_gm_meta: Optional[list[Any]] = None\n2443:         self._orig_gm_lineno_map: Optional[dict[int, Optional[int]]] = None\n2444:         self._orig_gm_firstlineno: Optional[int] = None\n2445:         # Each SubgraphTracer is associated with a source target, which indicates\n2446:         # which operator this subgraph is attached to. We compute a source_fn_stack\n2447:         # based on the source target. For the root tracer, it's set to [].\n2448:         # This is useful for debugging and transforming the exported graph.\n2449:         if self.parent is None:\n2450:             self.source_fn_stack: list[Any] = []\n2451: ",

            "content": "404:         # An ND-matmul is reshape -> mm -> reshape sequence. We first replace\n405:         # the second reshape node with `new_node`. Then, we ensure that the\n406:         # original mm node in the sequence ends up with zero users by replacing\n407:         # it with a reverse reshape of `new_node`.\n408:         graph = new_node.graph\n409:         assert len(self.nodes) == 3\n410:         mm_node = self.nodes[1]\n411:         output_reshape_node = self.nodes[2]\n412: \n413:         assert mm_node.target in (aten.mm.default, aten._scaled_mm.default)\n414:         assert output_reshape_node.target == aten.reshape.default\n415: \n416:         output_reshape_node.replace_all_uses_with(new_node)\n417:         if len(mm_node.users) > 1:\n418:             with graph.inserting_after(new_node):\n419:                 new_mm_node = graph.call_function(\n420:                     aten.reshape.default,\n421:                     args=(new_node, list(_get_tensor(mm_node).shape)),\n422:                 )\n423: ...             "content": "646: \n647: def _insert_dequantize_node(node: Node, graph: Graph) -> None:\n648:     \"\"\"Inserts dequantize node for `node` in `graph`\"\"\"\n649:     with graph.inserting_after(node):\n650:         dequantize_node = graph.call_method(\"dequantize\", (node,))\n651:         for user_node in dict(node.users):\n652:             if user_node is not dequantize_node:\n653:                 user_node.replace_input_with(node, dequantize_node)\n654: \n655: ",
            "endLine": 655,

          },
          {
            "content": "507: # after custom modules in general, and replace these with \"quantize\" and \"dequantize\" nodes respectively.\n508: def _replace_observer_or_dequant_stub_with_dequantize_node(\n509:     node: Node, graph: Graph\n510: ) -> None:\n511:     call_custom_module_node = node.args[0]\n512:     assert isinstance(call_custom_module_node, Node), (\n513:         f\"Expecting the for call custom module node to be a Node, but got {call_custom_module_node}\"\n514:     )\n515:     node.replace_all_uses_with(call_custom_module_node)\n516:     graph.erase_node(node)\n517:     _insert_dequantize_node(call_custom_module_node, graph)\n518: \n519: ",

32. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 1100,
  "path": "torch/fx/graph.py",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
  "startLine": 996
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"996: @compatibility(is_backward_compatible=True)\\n997: class Graph:\\n998:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n999:     ``Graph`` is the main data structure used in the FX Intermediate Representation.\\n1000:     It consists of a series of ``Node`` s, each representing callsites (or other\\n1001:     syntactic constructs). The list of ``Node`` s, taken together, constitute a\\n1002:     valid Python function.\\n1003: \\n1004:     For example, the following code\\n1005: \\n1006:     .. code-block:: python\\n1007: \\n1008:         import torch\\n1009:         import torch.fx\\n1010: \\n1011: \\n1012:         class MyModule(torch.nn.Module):\\n1013:             def __init__(self):\\n1014:                 super().__init__()\\n1015:                 self.param = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.rand(3, 4))\\n1016:                 self.linear = torch.nn.Linear(4, 5)\\n1017: \\n1018:             def forward(self, x):\\n1019:                 return torch.topk(\\n1020:                     torch.sum(self.linear(x + self.linear.weight).relu(), dim=-1), 3\\n1021:                 )\\n1022: \\n1023: \\n1024:         m = MyModule()\\n1025:         gm = torch.fx.symbolic_trace(m)\\n1026: \\n1027:     Will produce the following Graph::\\n1028: \\n1029:         print(gm.graph)\\n1030: \\n1031:     .. code-block:: text\\n1032: \\n1033:         graph(x):\\n1034:             %linear_weight : [num_users=1] = self.linear.weight\\n1035:             %add_1 : [num_users=1] = call_function[target=operator.add](args = (%x, %linear_weight), kwargs = {})\\n1036:             %linear_1 : [num_users=1] = call_module[target=linear](args = (%add_1,), kwargs = {})\\n1037:             %relu_1 : [num_users=1] = call_method[target=relu](args = (%linear_1,), kwargs = {})\\n1038:             %sum_1 : [num_users=1] = call_function[target=torch.sum](args = (%relu_1,), kwargs = {dim: -1})\\n1039:             %topk_1 : [num_users=1] = call_function[target=torch.topk](args = (%sum_1, 3), kwargs = {})\\n1040:             return topk_1\\n1041: \\n1042:     For the semantics of operations represented in the ``Graph``, please see :class:`Node`.\\n1043:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1044: \\n1045:     @compatibility(is_backward_compatible=True)\\n1046:     def __init__(\\n1047:         self,\\n1048:         owning_module: Optional[\\\"GraphModule\\\"] = None,\\n1049:         tracer_cls: Optional[type[\\\"Tracer\\\"]] = None,\\n1050:         tracer_extras: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,\\n1051:     ):\\n1052:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1053:         Construct an empty Graph.\\n1054:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1055:         self._root: Node = Node(self, \\\"\\\", \\\"root\\\", \\\"\\\", (), {})\\n1056:         self._used_names: dict[str, int] = {}  # base name -> number\\n1057:         self._insert = self._root.prepend\\n1058:         self._len = 0\\n1059:         self._graph_namespace = _Namespace()\\n1060:         self._owning_module = owning_module\\n1061:         self._tracer_cls = tracer_cls\\n1062:         self._tracer_extras = tracer_extras\\n1063:         self._codegen = CodeGen()\\n1064:         self._co_fields: dict[str, Any] = {}\\n1065:         self._find_nodes_lookup_table = _FindNodesLookupTable()\\n1066: \\n1067:     @property\\n1068:     def owning_module(self):\\n1069:         return self._owning_module\\n1070: \\n1071:     @owning_module.setter\\n1072:     def owning_module(self, mod: Optional[\\\"GraphModule\\\"]):\\n1073:         self._owning_module = mod\\n1074: \\n1075:     @property\\n1076:     def nodes(self) -> _node_list:\\n1077:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1078:         Get the list of Nodes that constitute this Graph.\\n1079: \\n1080:         Note that this ``Node`` list representation is a doubly-linked list. Mutations\\n1081:         during iteration (e.g. delete a Node, add a Node) are safe.\\n1082: \\n1083:         Returns:\\n1084: \\n1085:             A doubly-linked list of Nodes. Note that ``reversed`` can be called on\\n1086:             this list to switch iteration order.\\n1087:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1088:         return _node_list(self)\\n1089: \\n1090:     @compatibility(is_backward_compatible=False)\\n1091:     def output_node(self) -> Node:\\n1092:         output_node = next(iter(reversed(self.nodes)))\\n1093:         assert output_node.op == \\\"output\\\"\\n1094:         return output_node\\n1095: \\n1096:     @compatibility(is_backward_compatible=False)\\n1097:     def find_nodes(\\n1098:         self, *, op: str, target: Optional[\\\"Target\\\"] = None, sort: bool = True\\n1099:     ):\\n1100:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/fx/graph.py%3FL996-1100\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "996: @compatibility(is_backward_compatible=True)\n997: class Graph:\n998:     \"\"\"\n999:     ``Graph`` is the main data structure used in the FX Intermediate Representation.\n1000:     It consists of a series of ``Node`` s, each representing callsites (or other\n1001:     syntactic constructs). The list of ``Node`` s, taken together, constitute a\n1002:     valid Python function.\n1003: \n1004:     For example, the following code\n1005: \n1006:     .. code-block:: python\n1007: \n1008:         import torch\n1009:         import torch.fx\n1010: \n1011: \n1012:         class MyModule(torch.nn.Module):\n1013:             def __init__(self):\n1014:                 super().__init__()\n1015:                 self.param = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.rand(3, 4))\n1016:                 self.linear = torch.nn.Linear(4, 5)\n1017: \n1018:             def forward(self, x):\n1019:                 return torch.topk(\n1020:                     torch.sum(self.linear(x + self.linear.weight).relu(), dim=-1), 3\n1021:                 )\n1022: \n1023: \n1024:         m = MyModule()\n1025:         gm = torch.fx.symbolic_trace(m)\n1026: \n1027:     Will produce the following Graph::\n1028: \n1029:         print(gm.graph)\n1030: \n1031:     .. code-block:: text\n1032: \n1033:         graph(x):\n1034:             %linear_weight : [num_users=1] = self.linear.weight\n1035:             %add_1 : [num_users=1] = call_function[target=operator.add](args = (%x, %linear_weight), kwargs = {})\n1036:             %linear_1 : [num_users=1] = call_module[target=linear](args = (%add_1,), kwargs = {})\n1037:             %relu_1 : [num_users=1] = call_method[target=relu](args = (%linear_1,), kwargs = {})\n1038:             %sum_1 : [num_users=1] = call_function[target=torch.sum](args = (%relu_1,), kwargs = {dim: -1})\n1039:             %topk_1 : [num_users=1] = call_function[target=torch.topk](args = (%sum_1, 3), kwargs = {})\n1040:             return topk_1\n1041: \n1042:     For the semantics of operations represented in the ``Graph``, please see :class:`Node`.\n1043:     \"\"\"\n1044: \n1045:     @compatibility(is_backward_compatible=True)\n1046:     def __init__(\n1047:         self,\n1048:         owning_module: Optional[\"GraphModule\"] = None,\n1049:         tracer_cls: Optional[type[\"Tracer\"]] = None,\n1050:         tracer_extras: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,\n1051:     ):\n1052:         \"\"\"\n1053:         Construct an empty Graph.\n1054:         \"\"\"\n1055:         self._root: Node = Node(self, \"\", \"root\", \"\", (), {})\n1056:         self._used_names: dict[str, int] = {}  # base name -> number\n1057:         self._insert = self._root.prepend\n1058:         self._len = 0\n1059:         self._graph_namespace = _Namespace()\n1060:         self._owning_module = owning_module\n1061:         self._tracer_cls = tracer_cls\n1062:         self._tracer_extras = tracer_extras\n1063:         self._codegen = CodeGen()\n1064:         self._co_fields: dict[str, Any] = {}\n1065:         self._find_nodes_lookup_table = _FindNodesLookupTable()\n1066: \n1067:     @property\n1068:     def owning_module(self):\n1069:         return self._owning_module\n1070: \n1071:     @owning_module.setter\n1072:     def owning_module(self, mod: Optional[\"GraphModule\"]):\n1073:         self._owning_module = mod\n1074: \n1075:     @property\n1076:     def nodes(self) -> _node_list:\n1077:         \"\"\"\n1078:         Get the list of Nodes that constitute this Graph.\n1079: \n1080:         Note that this ``Node`` list representation is a doubly-linked list. Mutations\n1081:         during iteration (e.g. delete a Node, add a Node) are safe.\n1082: \n1083:         Returns:\n1084: \n1085:             A doubly-linked list of Nodes. Note that ``reversed`` can be called on\n1086:             this list to switch iteration order.\n1087:         \"\"\"\n1088:         return _node_list(self)\n1089: \n1090:     @compatibility(is_backward_compatible=False)\n1091:     def output_node(self) -> Node:\n1092:         output_node = next(iter(reversed(self.nodes)))\n1093:         assert output_node.op == \"output\"\n1094:         return output_node\n1095: \n1096:     @compatibility(is_backward_compatible=False)\n1097:     def find_nodes(\n1098:         self, *, op: str, target: Optional[\"Target\"] = None, sort: bool = True\n1099:     ):\n1100:         \"\"\"\n1101: ",

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1553:     def cleanup_graph(self) -> None:\\n1554:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1555:         Remove \\\"creation_timestamp\\\" from node meta\\n1556: \\n1557:         Remove this pattern from the graph:\\n1558:             torch._C._set_grad_enabled(False)\\n1559:             torch._C._set_grad_enabled(True)\\n1560:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1561:         assert self.should_exit\\n1562:         nodes = list(self.graph.nodes)\\n1563:         for node in nodes:\\n1564:             node.meta.pop(\\\"creation_timestamp\\\", None)\\n1565: \\n1566:         grad_enabled = torch.is_grad_enabled()\\n1567:         for node1, node2 in zip(nodes, nodes[1:]):\\n1568:             if (\\n1569:                 node1.target is torch._C._set_grad_enabled\\n1570:                 and tuple(node1.args) == (not grad_enabled,)\\n1571:                 and not node1._erased\\n1572:             ):\\n1573:                 grad_enabled = node1.args[0]\\n1574:                 if (\\n1575:                     node2.target is torch._C._set_grad_enabled\\n1576:                     and tuple(node2.args) == (not grad_enabled,)\\n1577:                     and not node2._erased\\n1578:                 ):\\n1579:                     grad_enabled = node2.args[0]\\n1580:                     self.graph.erase_node(node1)\\n1581:                     self.graph.erase_node(node2)\\n1582: \\n1583:     def get_graph_sizes_structured(self) -> dict[str, list[Union[int, str]]]:\\n1584:         ret: dict[str, list[Union[int, str]]] = {}\\n1585:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\\n1586: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL1553-1585\"}",
  "structuredContent": {

36. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "# PyTorch Graph Cleanup Fix\n\n## Issue\nFix #169388: Cleanup graphs for failed tracer outputs\n\n## Reference Cycles\n1. Graph nodes form a doubly-linked list (_prev, _next pointers) and point back to the graph\n2. FakeTensorMode \u2192 ShapeEnv \u2192 TrackedFake \u2192 FakeTensor \u2192 FakeTensorMode cycle\n\n## Files to Modify\n1. `torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py` - Enhance cleanup_graph() method\n\n## Solution\nThe cleanup_graph() method should:\n1. Break the node doubly-linked list by clearing _prev and _next pointers\n2. Clear shape_env.tracked_fakes list to break FakeTensorMode cycle\n\n## Key Classes/Methods\n- OutputGraph.cleanup_graph() - already exists, needs enhancement\n- OutputGraph.add_cleanup_hook() - registers cleanup functions\n- ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes - list of TrackedFake objects\n- SubgraphTracer.graph - the fx.Graph containing nodes\n- Node._prev, Node._next - doubly-linked list pointers\n",
  "file_path": "/logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/memory/MEMORY.md"
}
Output
{
  "content": "# PyTorch Graph Cleanup Fix\n\n## Issue\nFix #169388: Cleanup graphs for failed tracer outputs\n\n## Reference Cycles\n1. Graph nodes form a doubly-linked list (_prev, _next pointers) and point back to the graph\n2. FakeTensorMode \u2192 ShapeEnv \u2192 TrackedFake \u2192 FakeTensor \u2192 FakeTensorMode cycle\n\n## Files to Modify\n1. `torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py` - Enhance cleanup_graph() method\n\n## Solution\nThe cleanup_graph() method should:\n1. Break the node doubly-linked list by clearing _prev and _next pointers\n2. Clear shape_env.tracked_fakes list to break FakeTensorMode cycle\n\n## Key Classes/Methods\n- OutputGraph.cleanup_graph() - already exists, needs enhancement\n- OutputGraph.add_cleanup_hook() - registers cleanup functions\n- ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes - list of TrackedFake objects\n- SubgraphTracer.graph - the fx.Graph containing nodes\n- Node._prev, Node._next - doubly-linked list pointers\n",
  "filePath": "/logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/memory/MEMORY.md",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],
  "type": "create"
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2: Core graph building functionality for PyTorch's Dynamo system. This module contains\\n3: the essential components for constructing and managing FX graphs during compilation:\\n4: \\n5: - OutputGraph: Manages the overall graph construction and compilation process. It owns\\n6:   a SubgraphTracer and handles graph compilation, execution, and state management.\\n7:   OutputGraph also manages features like graph deduplication, symbolic shape handling,\\n8:   and tracking of side effects.\\n9: \\n10: - SubgraphTracer: Handles the actual FX graph construction by tracing Python code.\\n11:   It supports advanced features like higher-order operators through nested tracers,\\n12:   lifting of free variables, and handling of symbolic shapes.\\n13: \\n14: The module supports key Dynamo features including:\\n15: - Higher-order operators through nested SubgraphTracers\\n16: - Graph deduplication for optimization\\n17: - Symbolic shape handling and propagation\\n18: - Side effect tracking and management\\n19: - Guard insertion and management\\n20: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n21: \\n22: import collections\\n23: import contextlib\\n24: import copy\\n25: import functools\\n26: import inspect\\n27: import itertools\\n28: import logging\\n29: import operator\\n30: import re\\n31: import sys\\n32: import traceback\\n33: import warnings\\n34: import weakref\\n35: from collections.abc import Generator, Sequence\\n36: from dataclasses import dataclass, field as dc_field\\n37: from types import CodeType\\n38: from typing import Any, Callable, cast, Optional, TYPE_CHECKING, Union\\n39: from typing_extensions import ParamSpec, TypeVar\\n40: \\n41: import sympy\\n42: \\n43: import torch._guards\\n44: import torch._logging\\n45: import torch.distributed as dist\\n46: import torch.nn\\n47: import torch.utils._pytree as pytree\\n48: from torch import fx, Tensor\\n49: from torch._C._dynamo import guards\\n50: from torch._dynamo.exc import ShortenTraceback, TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis\\n51: from torch._guards import (\\n52:     CompileContext,\\n53:     CompileId,\\n54:     GlobalContextCheckpointState,\\n55:     Source,\\n56:     tracing,\\n57:     TracingContext,\\n58: )\\n59: from torch._subclasses.fake_tensor import FakeTensor\\n60: from torch._utils_internal import signpost_event\\n61: from torch.export.dynamic_shapes import _ConstraintTarget\\n62: from torch.fx._lazy_graph_module import _make_graph_module  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n63: from torch.fx.experimental._backward_state import BackwardState\\n64: from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import (\\n65:     free_symbols,\\n66:     guard_scalar,\\n67:     is_symbolic,\\n68:     ShapeEnv,\\n69:     Specialization,\\n70: )\\n71: from torch.fx.node import Target\\n72: from torch.fx.passes.runtime_assert import insert_deferred_runtime_asserts\\n73: from torch.multiprocessing.reductions import StorageWeakRef\\n74: from torch.utils._ordered_set import OrderedSet\\n75: from torch.utils._python_dispatch import is_traceable_wrapper_subclass\\n76: \\n77: from . import config, exc, logging as torchdynamo_logging, variables\\n78: from .backends.registry import CompiledFn, CompilerFn\\n79: from .bytecode_transformation import (\\n80:     create_call_function,\\n81:     create_instruction,\\n82:     create_load_const,\\n83:     Instruction,\\n84:     unique_id,\\n85: )\\n86: from .code_context import code_context\\n87: from .codegen import PyCodegen\\n88: from .current_scope_id import enter_new_scope\\n89: from .device_interface import get_interface_for_device\\n90: from .exc import (\\n91:     BackendCompilerFailed,\\n92:     exceptions_allowed_to_be_fallback,\\n93:     SkipFrame,\\n94:     unimplemented_v2,\\n95:     unimplemented_v2_with_warning,\\n96: )\\n97: from .graph_deduplication import apply_graph_deduplication\\n98: from .graph_region_tracker import GraphRegionTracker\\n99: from .guards import GuardBuilder, install_guard\\n100: from .mutation_guard import is_dynamic_nn_module\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL1-100\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: \"\"\"\n2: Core graph building functionality for PyTorch's Dynamo system. This module contains\n3: the essential components for constructing and managing FX graphs during compilation:\n4: \n5: - OutputGraph: Manages the overall graph construction and compilation process. It owns\n6:   a SubgraphTracer and handles graph compilation, execution, and state management.\n7:   OutputGraph also manages features like graph deduplication, symbolic shape handling,\n8:   and tracking of side effects.\n9: \n10: - SubgraphTracer: Handles the actual FX graph construction by tracing Python code.\n11:   It supports advanced features like higher-order operators through nested tracers,\n12:   lifting of free variables, and handling of symbolic shapes.\n13: \n14: The module supports key Dynamo features including:\n15: - Higher-order operators through nested SubgraphTracers\n16: - Graph deduplication for optimization\n17: - Symbolic shape handling and propagation\n18: - Side effect tracking and management\n19: - Guard insertion and management\n20: \"\"\"\n21: \n22: import collections\n23: import contextlib\n24: import copy\n25: import functools\n26: import inspect\n27: import itertools\n28: import logging\n29: import operator\n30: import re\n31: import sys\n32: import traceback\n33: import warnings\n34: import weakref\n35: from collections.abc import Generator, Sequence\n36: from dataclasses import dataclass, field as dc_field\n37: from types import CodeType\n38: from typing import Any, Callable, cast, Optional, TYPE_CHECKING, Union\n39: from typing_extensions import ParamSpec, TypeVar\n40: \n41: import sympy\n42: \n43: import torch._guards\n44: import torch._logging\n45: import torch.distributed as dist\n46: import torch.nn\n47: import torch.utils._pytree as pytree\n48: from torch import fx, Tensor\n49: from torch._C._dynamo import guards\n50: from torch._dynamo.exc import ShortenTraceback, TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis\n51: from torch._guards import (\n52:     CompileContext,\n53:     CompileId,\n54:     GlobalContextCheckpointState,\n55:     Source,\n56:     tracing,\n57:     TracingContext,\n58: )\n59: from torch._subclasses.fake_tensor import FakeTensor\n60: from torch._utils_internal import signpost_event\n61: from torch.export.dynamic_shapes import _ConstraintTarget\n62: from torch.fx._lazy_graph_module import _make_graph_module  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n63: from torch.fx.experimental._backward_state import BackwardState\n64: from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import (\n65:     free_symbols,\n66:     guard_scalar,\n67:     is_symbolic,\n68:     ShapeEnv,\n69:     Specialization,\n70: )\n71: from torch.fx.node import Target\n72: from torch.fx.passes.runtime_assert import insert_deferred_runtime_asserts\n73: from torch.multiprocessing.reductions import StorageWeakRef\n74: from torch.utils._ordered_set import OrderedSet\n75: from torch.utils._python_dispatch import is_traceable_wrapper_subclass\n76: \n77: from . import config, exc, logging as torchdynamo_logging, variables\n78: from .backends.registry import CompiledFn, CompilerFn\n79: from .bytecode_transformation import (\n80:     create_call_function,\n81:     create_instruction,\n82:     create_load_const,\n83:     Instruction,\n84:     unique_id,\n85: )\n86: from .code_context import code_context\n87: from .codegen import PyCodegen\n88: from .current_scope_id import enter_new_scope\n89: from .device_interface import get_interface_for_device\n90: from .exc import (\n91:     BackendCompilerFailed,\n92:     exceptions_allowed_to_be_fallback,\n93:     SkipFrame,\n94:     unimplemented_v2,\n95:     unimplemented_v2_with_warning,\n96: )\n97: from .graph_deduplication import apply_graph_deduplication\n98: from .graph_region_tracker import GraphRegionTracker\n99: from .guards import GuardBuilder, install_guard\n100: from .mutation_guard import is_dynamic_nn_module\n101: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL1-100"
  }
}

Exit from all context manager variables to make sure global state is restored\\n1333:         for block in reversed(self.root_tx.block_stack):\\n1334:             block.exit(self.root_tx, is_graph_break=reason.graph_break)\\n1335: \\n1336:         self.cleanup_graph()\\n1337: \\n1338:         # stack values and restore vars for each frame are pushed in reverse order\\n1339:         # i.e. last element corresponds to root frame, first element corresponds to current frame\\n1340:         all_stack_values = []\\n1341:         all_restore_vars = []\\n1342:         all_stack_locals_metas = []\\n1343:         cur_tx: Optional[InstructionTranslatorBase] = tx\\n1344:         while True:\\n1345:             assert cur_tx is not None\\n1346:             #
