  "content": "{\"content\":\"388:     def __init__(\\n389:         self,\\n390:         code_options: dict[str, Any],\\n391:         compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn],\\n392:         root_tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\",\\n393:         export: bool,\\n394:         export_constraints: Sequence[_ConstraintTarget],\\n395:         frame_state: Any,\\n396:         local_scope: Scope,\\n397:         global_scope: Scope,\\n398:         f_code: CodeType,\\n399:         torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode],\\n400:         package: Optional[\\\"CompilePackage\\\"],\\n401:     ) -> None:\\n402:         super().__init__(\\n403:             local_scope,\\n404:             global_scope,\\n405:             torch_function_mode_stack,\\n406:             guard_on_key_order=set(),\\n407:             input_source_to_sizes_strides={},\\n408:             dual_level=torch.autograd.forward_ad._current_level,\\n409:             functorch_layers=torch._functorch.pyfunctorch.retrieve_all_functorch_interpreters(),\\n410:             current_device=torch.utils._device.CURRENT_DEVICE,\\n411:             # initial_global_state is only None during NopTest.\\n412:             global_state_guard=torch._dynamo.convert_frame.initial_global_state\\n413:             or torch._C._dynamo.guards.GlobalStateGuard(),\\n414:             # These are set by @property instead, just initialize them as blank\\n415:             _guards=torch._guards.GuardsSet(),\\n416:             _aotautograd_guards=[],\\n417:         )\\n418:         self.tracers = [SubgraphTracer(self, is_export=export)]\\n419:         # Map from graph input's `Source` to its `VariableTracker` to\\n420:         # de-duplicate graph inputs by source and reuse the tracker\\n421:         self.input_source_to_var: dict[Source, VariableTracker] = {}\\n422:         self.export = export\\n423:         self.export_constraints = export_constraints  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n424:         self.frame_state = frame_state\\n425:         self.cleanup_hooks: list[Callable[[], Any]] = []\\n426:         # compile_id is an id number for the current torch.compile\\n427:         self.compile_id: int = next(_compile_id_counter)\\n428:         # Set of globals installed via install_global* APIs\\n429:         self.installed_globals: set[str] = set()\\n430: \\n431:         # TODO: maybe should just pass the entire f_code in here?  Not\\n432:         # sure...\\n433:         self.co_fields = {\\n434:             \\\"co_name\\\": f_code.co_name,\\n435:             \\\"co_filename\\\": f_code.co_filename,\\n436:             \\\"co_firstlineno\\\": f_code.co_firstlineno,\\n437:         }\\n438: \\n439:         self.region_tracker = GraphRegionTracker()\\n440: \\n441:         # tracked_fakes says where any tensor that was wrapped to fake came\\n442:         # from.  It is similar to GraphArg, in that all GraphArgs will get\\n443:         # will get added to TrackedFakes, but TrackedFakes also contains\\n444:         # GraphArgs that got pruned, and things like Tensor attributes which\\n445:         # aren't explicit graph inputs.  Used by shape guard\\n446:         self.tracked_fakes: list[TrackedFake] = []\\n447: \\n448:         shape_env = ShapeEnv(\\n449:             # Reference Cycle!\\n450:             # Share a reference to the list of TrackedFake.\\n451:             #\\n452:             # ShapeEnv needs this in order to be able to reproduce the call\\n453:             # to produce_guards at an arbitrary time point. That is because\\n454:             # TrackedFake instances may have its metadata changed throughout\\n455:             # the program execution.\\n456:             tracked_fakes=self.tracked_fakes,\\n457:             allow_scalar_outputs=config.capture_scalar_outputs,\\n458:             allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=config.capture_dynamic_output_shape_ops,\\n459:             prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards=config.prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards,\\n460:             allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts=config.allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts,\\n461:             co_fields=self.co_fields,\\n462:         )\\n463: \\n464:         # In export mode, we force the shape_env to strictly disallow any constraining\\n465:         # of the user marked dynamic dims\\n466:         import torch._functorch.config as _config\\n467: \\n468:         with _config.patch(fake_tensor_allow_unsafe_data_ptr_access=False):\\n469:             fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(\\n470:                 shape_env=shape_env,\\n471:                 # TODO (tmanlaibaatar) Remove this once we always lift params and buffers\\n472:                 allow_non_fake_inputs=True if self.export else False,\\n473:                 export=self.export,\\n474:             )\\n475:         self.tracing_context: TracingContext = TracingContext(fake_mode)\\n476:         self.tracing_context.traced_code.append(f_code)\\n477:         self.dynamo_compile_id: Optional[CompileId] = (\\n478:             CompileContext.current_compile_id()\\n479:         )\\n480:         self.init_ambient_guards()\\n481: \\n482:         # Map each tensor id to a list of sources. This is necessary because\\n483:         # tensor ids cannot be recovered from tracked fakes (in general).\\n484:         # We use this map to interpret (i.e., check for violations of) constraints,\\n485:         # specifically equality constraints, which have shared tensor ids in them.\\n486:         # This map should also be generally useful, e.g., for (de)serialization.\\n487:         self.tracked_fakes_id_to_source: dict[int, list[Source]] = (\\n488:             collections.defaultdict(list)\\n489:         )\\n490:         # Stores the full fqn of a param or buffer to the relevant source.\\n491: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL388-490\"}",

  "content": "{\"content\":\"490:         # Stores the full fqn of a param or buffer to the relevant source.\\n491:         self.param_name_to_source: Optional[dict[str, Source]] = {}\\n492:         self.side_effects = SideEffects(self)\\n493:         # Cached variable trackers. This makes symbolic analysis of LOAD_GLOBAL\\n494:         # and LOAD_ATTR for same python objects free.\\n495:         self.variable_tracker_cache = VariableTrackerCache()\\n496:         self.unique_var_id = itertools.count()\\n497:         self.code_options: dict[str, Any] = dict(code_options)\\n498:         self.output_instructions: list[Instruction] = []\\n499:         # used to track nodes that are added between calls of copy_graphstate\\n500:         # and restore_graphstate\\n501:         self.timestamp = 0\\n502: \\n503:         # A list of register_finalizer_fns to apply to the output graph module\\n504:         self.register_finalizer_fns: list[Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]] = []\\n505: \\n506:         # Not checkpointed\\n507:         self.compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn] = compiler_fn\\n508:         self.root_tx = root_tx\\n509: \\n510:         self.package = package\\n511:         # Given a source, what are the user stacks of all locations that\\n512:         # accessed it?\\n513:         #\\n514:         # For efficiency, we only populate this:\\n515:         #   - During export, and\\n516:         #   - If the source could potentially lead to a spurious export input\\n517:         #\\n518:         # Feel free to populate this more frequently if other use-cases arise,\\n519:         # but be aware that we have to generate full stacks for each\\n520:         # recording!\\n521:         self.source_to_user_stacks: dict[Source, list[traceback.StackSummary]] = {}\\n522: \\n523:         self._current_tx: list[InstructionTranslatorBase] = []\\n524:         self.cleanups: list[CleanupHook] = []\\n525:         self.should_exit = False\\n526:         self.unspec_variable_map: dict[str, UnspecializedPythonVariable] = {}\\n527: \\n528:         # This returns false if TF Overall (both mode and subclass) is disabled OR that TF Mode stack is empty\\n529:         self.torch_function_mode_enabled = torch._C._is_torch_function_mode_enabled()\\n530: \\n531:         # Tracks if the output graph has a user defined allowed function in the\\n532:         # graph. This is used later to determine if we should fallback to eager\\n533:         # for certain exceptions. THe idea is that if the user has applied\\n534:         # allow_in_graph, they would like to see the error instead of falling\\n535:         # back for backend errors.\\n536:         self.has_user_defined_allowed_in_graph = False\\n537: \\n538:         # Tracks a list of called ops that were not tagged with \\\"pt2_compliant_tag\\\".\\n539:         # This information is useful for logging.\\n540:         self.non_compliant_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\\n541: \\n542:         # Tracks a list of called custom ops that were tagged with \\\"pt2_compliant_tag\\\".\\n543:         # This information is useful for logging.\\n544:         self.compliant_custom_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\\n545: \\n546:         # We save the global torch state here to be restored in case of graph\\n547:         # breaks. The relevant issue is seen here\\n548:         # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/100570#issuecomment-1543427086\\n549:         # where inlining of a function changes the global state (because of the\\n550:         # presence of torch.no_grad) and there is a graph break.\\n551: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL490-550\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "490:         # Stores the full fqn of a param or buffer to the relevant source.\n491:         self.param_name_to_source: Optional[dict[str, Source]] = {}\n492:         self.side_effects = SideEffects(self)\n493:         # Cached variable trackers. This makes symbolic analysis of LOAD_GLOBAL\n494:         # and LOAD_ATTR for same python objects free.\n495:         self.variable_tracker_cache = VariableTrackerCache()\n496:         self.unique_var_id = itertools.count()\n497:         self.code_options: dict[str, Any] = dict(code_options)\n498:         self.output_instructions: list[Instruction] = []\n499:         # used to track nodes that are added between calls of copy_graphstate\n500:         # and restore_graphstate\n501:         self.timestamp = 0\n502: \n503:         # A list of register_finalizer_fns to apply to the output graph module\n504:         self.register_finalizer_fns: list[Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]] = []\n505: \n506:         # Not checkpointed\n507:         self.compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn] = compiler_fn\n508:         self.root_tx = root_tx\n509: \n510:         self.package = package\n511:         # Given a source, what are the user stacks of all locations that\n512:         # accessed it?\n513:         #\n514:         # For efficiency, we only populate this:\n515:         #   - During export, and\n516:         #   - If the source could potentially lead to a spurious export input\n517:         #\n518:         # Feel free to populate this more frequently if other use-cases arise,\n519:         # but be aware that we have to generate full stacks for each\n520:         # recording!\n521:         self.source_to_user_stacks: dict[Source, list[traceback.StackSummary]] = {}\n522: \n523:         self._current_tx: list[InstructionTranslatorBase] = []\n524:         self.cleanups: list[CleanupHook] = []\n525:         self.should_exit = False\n526:         self.unspec_variable_map: dict[str, UnspecializedPythonVariable] = {}\n527: \n528:         # This returns false if TF Overall (both mode and subclass) is disabled OR that TF Mode stack is empty\n529:         self.torch_function_mode_enabled = torch._C._is_torch_function_mode_enabled()\n530: \n531:         # Tracks if the output graph has a user defined allowed function in the\n532:         # graph. This is used later to determine if we should fallback to eager\n533:         # for certain exceptions. THe idea is that if the user has applied\n534:         # allow_in_graph, they would like to see the error instead of falling\n535:         # back for backend errors.\n536:         self.has_user_defined_allowed_in_graph = False\n537: \n538:         # Tracks a list of called ops that were not tagged with \"pt2_compliant_tag\".\n539:         # This information is useful for logging.\n540:         self.non_compliant_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\n541: \n542:         # Tracks a list of called custom ops that were tagged with \"pt2_compliant_tag\".\n543:         # This information is useful for logging.\n544:         self.compliant_custom_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\n545: \n546:         # We save the global torch state here to be restored in case of graph\n547:         # breaks. The relevant issue is seen here\n548:         # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/100570#issuecomment-1543427086\n549:         # where inlining of a function changes the global state (because of the\n550:         # presence of torch.no_grad) and there is a graph break.\n551: ",

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"550:         # presence of torch.no_grad) and there is a graph break.\\n551:         self.save_global_state()\\n552: \\n553:         # Tracks the original FQNs of the constant tensors from the original graph,\\n554:         # i.e. buffers and parameters.\\n555:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn: dict[str, str] = {}\\n556: \\n557:         # All calls to random() are replaced with a single call to __gen_rand_values\\n558:         # functions that returns a tuple of random values for each original call.\\n559:         # random_calls tracks calls to random() and random_values_var stores the name of\\n560:         # the variable that stores __gen_rand_values results.\\n561:         self.random_calls: list[\\n562:             tuple[Callable[..., object], tuple[object, ...], dict[str, object]]\\n563:         ] = []\\n564:         self.random_values_var: Any = None\\n565: \\n566:         # Bytecode to insert right before we call the graph\\n567:         self.pregraph_bytecode: list[Instruction] = []\\n568: \\n569:         # Use to pass values to backward hooks when using compiled autograd\\n570:         self.backward_state: dict[str, VariableTracker] = {}\\n571:         self.backward_state_proxy: Optional[torch.fx.Proxy] = None\\n572:         self.backward_state_var: Optional[str] = None\\n573: \\n574:         self.name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals: str = (\\n575:             self.install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals()\\n576:         )\\n577: \\n578:         self.compiler_trace_stack = contextlib.ExitStack()\\n579: \\n580:         # These are the ambient, currently-global saved_tensor_hooks stashed in autograd,\\n581: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL550-580\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "550:         # presence of torch.no_grad) and there is a graph break.\n551:         self.save_global_state()\n552: \n553:         # Tracks the original FQNs of the constant tensors from the original graph,\n554:         # i.e. buffers and parameters.\n555:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn: dict[str, str] = {}\n556: \n557:         # All calls to random() are replaced with a single call to __gen_rand_values\n558:         # functions that returns a tuple of random values for each original call.\n559:         # random_calls tracks calls to random() and random_values_var stores the name of\n560:         # the variable that stores __gen_rand_values results.\n561:         self.random_calls: list[\n562:             tuple[Callable[..., object], tuple[object, ...], dict[str, object]]\n563:         ] = []\n564:         self.random_values_var: Any = None\n565: \n566:         # Bytecode to insert right before we call the graph\n567:         self.pregraph_bytecode: list[Instruction] = []\n568: \n569:         # Use to pass values to backward hooks when using compiled autograd\n570:         self.backward_state: dict[str, VariableTracker] = {}\n571:         self.backward_state_proxy: Optional[torch.fx.Proxy] = None\n572:         self.backward_state_var: Optional[str] = None\n573: \n574:         self.name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals: str = (\n575:             self.install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals()\n576:         )\n577: \n578:         self.compiler_trace_stack = contextlib.ExitStack()\n579: \n580:         # These are the ambient, currently-global saved_tensor_hooks stashed in autograd,\n581: ",

  "content": "{\"content\":\"575:             self.install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals()\\n576:         )\\n577: \\n578:         self.compiler_trace_stack = contextlib.ExitStack()\\n579: \\n580:         # These are the ambient, currently-global saved_tensor_hooks stashed in autograd,\\n581:         # that are set for the entire duration of the compiled region.\\n582:         # This is an invariant today because we graph break on the saved_tensor_hook\\n583:         # context manager inside a compiled region\\n584:         self.saved_tensors_hooks_subgraph_names: Optional[list[str]] = (\\n585:             self.maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs()\\n586:         )\\n587: \\n588:     def mark_bytecode_tracing_start(self) -> None:\\n589:         self.compiler_trace_stack.enter_context(\\n590:             dynamo_timed(\\n591:                 \\\"bytecode_tracing\\\",\\n592:                 log_pt2_compile_event=True,\\n593:             )\\n594:         )\\n595: \\n596:     def mark_bytecode_tracing_stop(self) -> None:\\n597:         self.compiler_trace_stack.close()\\n598: \\n599:     def install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals(self) -> str:\\n600:         f_builtins = get_builtins_dict(self.global_scope)\\n601:         return self.install_global(\\\"__builtins_dict__\\\", f_builtins)\\n602: \\n603:     def add_backward_state_hook(\\n604:         self, hook: VariableTracker, prefix: str = \\\"hook\\\"\\n605:     ) -> tuple[str, torch.fx.Proxy]:\\n606:         name = f\\\"{prefix}{len(self.backward_state)}\\\"\\n607:         assert name not in self.backward_state\\n608:         self.backward_state[name] = hook\\n609:         return name, self.get_backward_state_proxy()\\n610: \\n611:     def get_backward_state_proxy(self) -> torch.fx.Proxy:\\n612:         if self.backward_state_proxy is None:\\n613:             if self.export:\\n614:                 unimplemented_v2(\\n615:                     gb_type=\\\"backward_state does not support export\\\",\\n616:                     context=\\\"\\\",\\n617:                     explanation=\\\"Compiled autograd doesn't work with `torch.export`.\\\",\\n618:                     hints=[],\\n619:                 )\\n620:             example_value = BackwardState()\\n621:       "text": "Perfect! Now let me create the final comprehensive report:\n\n## Summary: Files to Modify for Cleaning Up Graphs for Failed Tracer Outputs\n\nBased on my thorough search of the `github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1` repository, here are the files that likely need to be modified, ordered by importance:\n\n### PRIMARY FILES (Core Implementation - Most Important)\n\n1. **/workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py** (HIGHEST PRIORITY)\n   - **cleanup_graph()** method (lines 1553-1570+): Removes creation timestamps and grad_enabled patterns from the FX graph\n   - **cleanup()** method (lines 2228-2248): Breaks reference cycles between tracer and OutputGraph by clearing caches (real_value_cache, input_name_to_proxy, side_effects, variable_tracker_cache, etc.)\n   - **add_cleanup_hook()** / **call_cleanup_hooks()** methods (lines 737-743): Manages cleanup callbacks for deferred cleanup operations\n   - **tracked_fakes initialization** (line 446): List of TrackedFake objects that need cleanup after failed tracing\n\n2. **/workspace/torch/_dynamo/symbolic_convert.py** (HIGH PRIORITY)\n   - **cleanup() call in finally block** (lines 1405-1413): Ensures OutputGraph cleanup happens for InstructionTranslator (but not InliningInstructionTranslator)\n   - **_failed flag and error handling** (lines 219-221): Tracks failed compilation states\n   - **Exception handling with cleanup instructions** (lines 960-975): Ensures cleanup bytecode is generated for exception handlers\n\n3. **/workspace/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py** (HIGH PRIORITY)\n   - **call_cleanup_hooks() in finally block** (lines 837-838): Calls cleanup hooks after tracing (even if exception occurs)\n   - **CleanupManager.instance assignment** (line 1012): Stores cleanup callbacks for compiled code objects\n   - **Exception handling patterns** (lines 830-850): Context where cleanup must be guaranteed\n\n### SECONDARY FILES (Related Infrastructure)\n\n4. **/workspace/torch/_dynamo/utils.py**\n   - **CleanupHook class** (lines 2046-2065): Dataclass for removing global variables on cleanup\n   - **CleanupManager class** (lines 2067-2077): Weak key dictionary managing cleanup callbacks per code object\n\n5. **/workspace/torch/_guards.py**\n   - **ShapeEnv cleanup in finally block** (lines 1053-1059): Cleans up shape environment to break reference cycles after tracing context\n\n6. **/workspace/torch/fx/experimental/symbolic_shapes.py**\n   - **TrackedFake class definition** (lines 2169-2187): Dataclass tracking sources of fake tensors with __hash__ and __eq__ methods\n   - **ShapeEnv.cleanup() method** (lines 7675-7688): Breaks reference cycles by cleaning up var_to_stack and deferred_runtime_asserts\n\n7. **/workspace/torch/_dynamo/variables/builder.py**\n   - **TrackedFake imports and usage** (lines 70-72, 1162, 2322-2324): Creates and appends TrackedFake instances for wrapped values\n\n### TEST FILES (Cleanup Testing - Important for Validation)\n\n8. **/workspace/test/dynamo/test_export.py**\n   - **test_export_dynamic_dim_cleanup()** (lines 3021-3028): Tests dynamic dimension cleanup during export\n   - Expected failures file: `/workspace/test/dynamo_expected_failures/ExportTests.test_export_dynamic_dim_cleanup`\n\n9. **/workspace/test/dynamo/test_exceptions.py**\n   - **test_block_stack_cleanup()** (lines 783-805): Tests exception handling with block stack cleanup\n   - Demonstrates the pattern of cleanup after exceptions in dynamically compiled code\n\n10. **/workspace/test/export/test_export.py**\n    - **test_cleanup_dynamic_markers()** (lines 7802-7804): Tests cleanup of dynamic markers in export\n\n### KEY RELATIONSHIPS\n\nThe cleanup flow for failed tracer outputs works as follows:\n\n1. **OutputGraph.cleanup()** (output_graph.py) - Main entry point, clears:\n   - tracked_fakes references\n   - real_value_cache\n   - input_name_to_proxy\n   - side_effects\n   - variable_tracker_cache\n   - tracing_context\n\n2. **Call cleanup hooks** (convert_frame.py & symbolic_convert.py) - Executes deferred cleanup operations registered via add_cleanup_hook()\n\n3. **ShapeEnv.cleanup()** (symbolic_shapes.py) - Breaks var_to_stack and deferred_runtime_asserts cycles\n\n4. **CleanupManager** (utils.py) - Manages cleanup callbacks per compiled code object\n\nAll critical files use try-finally patterns to ensure cleanup happens even when tracer fails, preventing reference cycles and memory leaks.",

    }

"{\"content\":\"1552: \\n1553:     def cleanup_graph(self) -> None:\\n1554:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1555:         Remove \\\"creation_timestamp\\\" from node meta\\n1556: \\n1557:         Remove this pattern from the graph:\\n1558:             torch._C._set_grad_enabled(False)\\n1559:             torch._C._set_grad_enabled(True)\\n1560:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1561:         assert self.should_exit\\n1562:         nodes = list(self.graph.nodes)\\n1563:         for node in nodes:\\n1564:             node.meta.pop(\\\"creation_timestamp\\\", None)\\n1565: \\n1566:         grad_enabled = torch.is_grad_enabled()\\n1567:         for node1, node2 in zip(nodes, nodes[1:]):\\n1568:             if (\\n1569:                 node1.target is torch._C._set_grad_enabled\\n1570:                 and tuple(node1.args) == (not grad_enabled,)\\n1571: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL1552-1570\"}"

"{\"content\":\"2228:     def cleanup(self) -> None:\\n2229:         # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\\n2230:         # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\\n2231:         self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n2232:         self.nn_modules.clear()\\n2233:         self.param_name_to_source = None\\n2234: \\n2235:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\\n2236:             if \\\"grapharg\\\" in node.meta:\\n2237:                 del node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"]\\n2238:         self.real_value_cache.clear()\\n2239:         self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\\n2240:         self.side_effects.clear()\\n2241:         self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\\n2242:         self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\\n2243:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\\n2244:         self.tracing_context.clear()\\n2245:         self.input_source_to_var.clear()\\n2246:         self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\\n2247:         self.backward_state.clear()\\n2248: \\n2249:     def add_graph_finalizer(\\n2250:         self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\\n2251:     ) -> None:\\n2252:         self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)\\n2253: \\n2254:     def example_value_from_input_node(self, node: torch.fx.Node) -> Any:\\n2255:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Extract the non-fake example tensor\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2256:         if node.op == \\\"placeholder\\\":\\n2257:             return node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"].example\\n2258:         assert node.op == \\\"get_attr\\\"\\n2259:         return self.nn_modules[node.target]  # type: ignore[index]\\n2260: \\n2261: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2228-2260\"}"

"{\"blocks\":[{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"3255: # Mechanically, we do the introspection by:\\n3256: # - Creating a new SubgraphTracer via OutputGraph.subtracer\\n3257: # - Executing the body function.\\n3258: # This constructs the graph of the body function in the new SubgraphTracer\\n3259: # while modifying the state of the OutputGraph. "{\"content\":\"3020: \\n3021:     def test_export_dynamic_dim_cleanup(self):\\n3022:         y = torch.randn([3, 3, 3])\\n3023: \\n3024:         def my_dyn_fn(x):\\n3025:             return x.cos()\\n3026: \\n3027:         torch._dynamo.export(my_dyn_fn, dynamic_shapes=({0: torch.export.Dim(\\\"dim\\\")},))(\\n3028:             y\\n3029:         )\\n3030: \\n3031:     @config.patch(capture_dynamic_output_shape_ops=True)\\n3032:     def test_export_dynamic_control_flow_error(self):\\n3033:         def f(x):\\n3034:             if x.nonzero() > 3:\\n3035:                 return x.cos()\\n3036:             return x.sin()\\n3037: \\n3038:         with self.assertRaisesRegex(\\n3039:             torch._dynamo.exc.Unsupported,\\n3040:             \\\"Data-dependent branching\\\",\\n3041:         ):\\n3042:             torch._dynamo.export(f, aten_graph=True)(torch.randn(5, 6))\\n3043: \\n3044:     @config.patch(assume_static_by_default=False)\\n3045:     def test_export_persist_assert(self):\\n3046:         def f(x):\\n3047:             assert x[0].sum() > 4, \\\"Shape must be more than 4\\\"\\n3048:             return x.cos() + x.sin()\\n3049: \\n3050:         gm, _ = torch._dynamo.export(f, aten_graph=True, tracing_mode=\\\"symbolic\\\")(\\n3051: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_export.py%3FL3020-3050\"}"

"{\"blocks\":[{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"4: bytecode right before it is executed. It rewrites Python bytecode in order to extract sequences of\\n5: PyTorch operations into an FX Graph which is then just-in-time compiled with a customizable backend.\\n6: It creates this FX Graph through bytecode analysis and is designed to mix Python execution with\\n7: compiled backends to get the best of both worlds: usability and performance. This allows it to\\n8: \",\"endLine\":8,\"startLine\":4},{\"content\":\"119:     NB: this does NOT reset logging state.  Don't use this to test logging\\n120:     initialization/reinitialization.\\n121:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n122: \",\"endLine\":122,\"startLine\":119},{\"content\":\"133:         guard_failures.clear()\\n134:         graph_break_reasons.clear()\\n135:         resume_execution.ContinueExecutionCache.cache.clear()\\n136:         _reset_guarded_backend_cache()\\n137: \",\"endLine\":137,\"startLine\":133}],\"file\":\"torch/_dynamo/__init__.py\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/__init__.py\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"9: - Handling tensor sources and guards for optimizer state tensors\\n10: - Supporting CUDA graph execution through static tensor address management\\n11: - Providing special handling for parameter "{\"blocks\":[{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"3: \\n4: \\\"\\\"\\\"Exception handling and error reporting for TorchDynamo.\\n5: \\n6: This module provides a comprehensive set of exception classes and utilities for error\\n7: handling in TorchDynamo. It includes:\\n8: \\n9: Base Exceptions:\\n10:     - TorchDynamoException: Base class for all TorchDynamo-specific exceptions\\n11:     - Various specialized subclasses for different error scenarios\\n12: \\n13: User Error Handling:\\n14:     - UserError: Exceptions for user-facing errors in TorchDynamo usage\\n15:     - UserErrorType: Enumeration of different categories of user errors\\n16:     - Formatted error messages with debugging information\\n17: \\n18: Observed Exceptions:\\n19:     - Classes for handling exceptions observed during tracing\\n20:     - Special handling for StopIteration, LookupError, etc.\\n21:     - Exception state management during compilation\\n22: \\n23: Error Formatting:\\n24:     - Stack trace filtering and formatting\\n25:     - Error message augmentation\\n26:     - Debugging utilities for error reporting\\n27: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n28: \",\"endLine\":28,\"startLine\":3},{\"content\":\"287: \\n288: class PackageError(TorchDynamoException):\\n289:     pass\\n290: \",\"endLine\":290,\"startLine\":287},{\"content\":\"67: \\n68: class TorchDynamoException(RuntimeError):\\n69:     pass\\n70: \",\"endLine\":70,\"startLine\":67}],\"file\":\"torch/_dynamo/exc.py\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/exc.py\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"331: \\n332: class ReluCompileError(Exception):\\n333:     pass\\n334: \",\"endLine\":334,\"startLine\":331},{\"content\":\"335: \\n336: class TestingOnlyCompileError(Exception):\\n337:     pass\\n338: \",\"endLine\":338,\"startLine\":335},{\"content\":\"19: These backends are primarily used for:\\n20: 1. that it should not compile the partial graph.\\\",\\n330:       \\\"Hints\\\": [\\n331:         \\\"Use `torch._assert()` to raise a hard AssertionError when the check fails. \\\",\\n332: assert statement.\\\",\\n335:         \\\"Move the assert statement outside of any context managers in order to graph break with \\\",\\n336:         \\\"partial graph compilation (if "{\"content\":\"7674: \\n7675:     def cleanup(self) -> None:\\n7676:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n7677:         Break reference cycles.\\n7678: \\n7679:         This destroys the stacks. If you really want to keep them, we\\n7680:         just need some way to break references on code objects.\\n7681:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n7682:         for s in self.var_to_stack.values():\\n7683:             s.cleanup()\\n7684:         for ras in self.deferred_runtime_asserts.values():\\n7685:             for ra in ras:\\n7686:                 ra.stack.cleanup()\\n7687: \\n7688:     @lru_cache(256)\\n7689:     @record_shapeenv_event(save_tracked_fakes=True)\\n7690:     def guard_or_defer_runtime_assert(\\n7691:         self, orig_expr: SympyBoolean, msg: str, fx_node: Optional[torch.fx.Node] = None\\n7692:     ) -> bool:\\n7693:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n7694:         Adds a guard that orig_expr is True if we can or fall back to adding an assert\\n7695:         that is checked at runtime.\\n7696: \\n7697:         Args:\\n7698:             orig_expr (sympy.Expr): Boolean expression to assert is true\\n7699:             msg (str): Message to display on assertion failure\\n7700:             fx_node (Optional, torch.fx.Node): node in ``self.graph`` corresponding\\n7701: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/fx/experimental/symbolic_shapes.py%3FL7674-7700\"}"

By default, the partitioner is purely trying to optimize for runtime (although\\n134: # it should always use less memory than eager)\\n135: # This knob controls the partitioner to make that tradeoff for you, choosing the\\n136: # fastest option that saves less activations than the memory budget.\\n137: # Specifically, 0.0 corresponds to the activation memory from applying\\n138: # activation checkpointing to the full compiled region, and 1.0 corresponds to\\n139: # the activation memory from the default runtime-optimized strategy. "{\"content\":\"1000:         assert code.co_cellvars == out_code.co_cellvars, msg\\n1001: \\n1002:         # Skipping Dynamo on a frame without any extracted graph.\\n1003:         # This does not affect eager functionality. But this is necessary\\n1004:         # for export for cases where Dynamo-reconstructed bytecode can create\\n1005:         # new function frames, confusing export in thinking that there\\n1006:         # are extra graphs now.\\n1007: \\n1008:         if output.export and output.is_empty_graph():\\n1009:             return ConvertFrameReturn()\\n1010: \\n1011:         assert output.guards is not None\\n1012:         CleanupManager.instance[out_code] = output.cleanups\\n1013:         nonlocal cache_entry\\n1014:         with dynamo_timed(\\\"build_guards\\\", log_pt2_compile_event=True):\\n1015:             check_fn = CheckFunctionManager(\\n1016:                 code,\\n1017:                 output,\\n1018:                 cache_entry,\\n1019:                 hooks.guard_fail_fn if hooks else None,\\n1020:                 hooks.guard_filter_fn if hooks else None,\\n1021: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py%3FL1000-1020\"}"

"{\"content\":\"2046: @dataclasses.dataclass\\n2047: class CleanupHook:\\n2048:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Remove a global variable when hook is called\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2049: \\n2050:     scope: dict[str, Any]\\n2051:     name: str\\n2052: \\n2053:     def __call__(self, *args: Any) -> None:\\n2054:         # Make sure we're not shutting down\\n2055:         if CleanupManager is not None:\\n2056:             CleanupManager.count -= 1\\n2057:         del self.scope[self.name]\\n2058: \\n2059:     @staticmethod\\n2060:     def create(scope: dict[str, Any], name: str, val: Any) -> CleanupHook:\\n2061:         assert name not in scope\\n2062:         CleanupManager.count += 1\\n2063:         scope[name] = val\\n2064:         return CleanupHook(scope, name)\\n2065: \\n2066: \\n2067: class CleanupManager(ExactWeakKeyDictionary):\\n2068:     count = 0\\n2069:     instance: ClassVar[CleanupManager]\\n2070: \\n2071:     def _remove_id(self, idx: int) -> None:\\n2072:         for hook in self.values[idx]:\\n2073:             hook()\\n2074:         super()._remove_id(idx)\\n2075: \\n2076: \\n2077: CleanupManager.instance = CleanupManager()\\n2078: \\n2079: \\n2080: def clone_tensor(x: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:\\n2081:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Clone the tensor and its gradient\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2082:     y = x.clone().requires_grad_(x.requires_grad)\\n2083:     if x.is_leaf and x.grad is not None:\\n2084:         y.grad = x.grad.clone()\\n2085:     return y\\n2086: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/utils.py%3FL2046-2085\"}"

"{\"content\":\"2169: @dataclass\\n2170: class TrackedFake:\\n2171:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2172:     Tracks the sources of all fake tensors we wrap in Dynamo.\\n2173:     Used by shape guard computation.\\n2174:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2175: \\n2176:     fake: Union[FakeTensor, SymInt]\\n2177:     source: Source\\n2178:     symbolic_context: Optional[SymbolicContext]\\n2179: \\n2180:     def __hash__(self) -> int:\\n2181:         return hash((self.fake, self.source.name()))\\n2182: \\n2183:     def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool:\\n2184:         if isinstance(other, TrackedFake):\\n2185:             return self.fake is other.fake and self.source.name() == other.source.name()\\n2186:         return False\\n2187: \\n2188: \\n2189: def is_symbolic(\\n2190:     val: Union[int, SymInt, float, SymFloat, bool, SymBool],\\n2191: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/fx/experimental/symbolic_shapes.py%3FL2169-2190\"}"

"{\"content\":\"440: \\n441:         # tracked_fakes says where any tensor that was wrapped to fake came\\n442:         # from.  It is similar to GraphArg, in that all GraphArgs will get\\n443:         # will get added to TrackedFakes, but TrackedFakes also contains\\n444:         # GraphArgs that got pruned, and things like Tensor attributes which\\n445:         # aren't explicit graph inputs.  Used by shape guard\\n446:         self.tracked_fakes: list[TrackedFake] = []\\n447: \\n448:         shape_env = ShapeEnv(\\n449:             # Reference Cycle!\\n450:             # Share a reference to the list of TrackedFake.\\n451:             #\\n452:             # ShapeEnv needs this in order to be able to reproduce the call\\n453:             # to produce_guards at an arbitrary time point. That is because\\n454:             # TrackedFake instances may have its metadata changed throughout\\n455:             # the program execution.\\n456:             tracked_fakes=self.tracked_fakes,\\n457:             allow_scalar_outputs=config.capture_scalar_outputs,\\n458:             allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=config.capture_dynamic_output_shape_ops,\\n459:             prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards=config.prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards,\\n460:             allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts=config.allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts,\\n461: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL440-460\"}"

"{\"content\":\"782: \\n783:     def test_block_stack_cleanup(self):\\n784:         params = {\\n785:             \\\"a\\\": 3,\\n786:             \\\"b\\\": 4,\\n787:             \\\"c\\\": 5,\\n788:         }\\n789: \\n790:         dt = {\\n791:             \\\"c\\\": 5,\\n792:         }\\n793: \\n794:         def fn(x):\\n795:             for name in params:\\n796:                 try:\\n797:                     x = x * dt[name]\\n798:                 except KeyError:\\n799:                     x = x * torch.sin(x)\\n800:             return x\\n801: \\n802:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\\\"eager\\\", fullgraph=True)\\n803:         x = torch.randn(4)\\n804:         self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\\n805: \\n806:     def test_set_cause_with_arg(self):\\n807:         @torch.compile(backend=\\\"eager\\\", fullgraph=True)\\n808:         def fn(t, err):\\n809:             err.__cause__ = ValueError()\\n810:             return t.sin()\\n811: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_exceptions.py%3FL782-810\"}"

{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"2227: \\n2228:     def cleanup(self) -> None:\\n2229:         # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\\n2230:         # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\\n2231:         self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n2232:         self.nn_modules.clear()\\n2233:         self.param_name_to_source = None\\n2234: \\n2235:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\\n2236:             if \\\"grapharg\\\" in node.meta:\\n2237:                 del node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"]\\n2238:         self.real_value_cache.clear()\\n2239:         self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\\n2240:         self.side_effects.clear()\\n2241:         self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\\n2242:         self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\\n2243:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\\n2244:         self.tracing_context.clear()\\n2245:         self.input_source_to_var.clear()\\n2246:         self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\\n2247:         self.backward_state.clear()\\n2248: \\n2249:     def add_graph_finalizer(\\n2250:         self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\\n2251:     ) -> None:\\n2252:         self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)\\n2253: \\n2254:     def example_value_from_input_node(self, node: torch.fx.Node) -> Any:\\n2255:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Extract the non-fake example tensor\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2256:         if node.op == \\\"placeholder\\\":\\n2257:             return node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"].example\\n2258:         assert node.op == \\\"get_attr\\\"\\n2259:         return self.nn_modules[node.target]  # type: ignore[index]\\n2260: \\n2261: \\n2262: err_epilogue = (\\n2263:     \\\"With the current config, we will graph break \\\"\\n2264:     \\\"(and fall back to eager-mode PyTorch) on all ops \\\"\\n2265:     \\\"that have do not have the 'pt2_compliant_tag'. \\\"\\n2266:     \\\"Please see the following doc for how to mark this op as PT2 compliant \\\"\\n2267:     \\\"https://pytorch.org/tutorials/advanced/custom_ops_landing_page.html\\\"\\n2268: )\\n2269: \\n2270: \\n2271: def check_pt2_compliant_op(\\n2272:     output_graph: OutputGraph, kind: str, target: Any, args: Any, kwargs: Any\\n2273: ) -> None:\\n2274:     if kind != \\\"call_function\\\":\\n2275:         return\\n2276: \\n2277:     def encountered_compliant_op(target: torch._ops.OpOverload) -> None:\\n2278:         if target.namespace in {\\\"prim\\\", \\\"prims\\\", \\\"aten\\\"}:\\n2279:             return\\n2280:         output_graph.compliant_custom_ops.add(target)\\n2281: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2227-2280\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "2227: \n2228:     def cleanup(self) -> None:\n2229:         # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\n2230:         # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\n2231:         self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\n2232:         self.nn_modules.clear()\n2233:         self.param_name_to_source = None\n2234: \n2235:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\n2236:             if \"grapharg\" in node.meta:\n2237:                 del node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n2238:         self.real_value_cache.clear()\n2239:         self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\n2240:         self.side_effects.clear()\n2241:         self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\n2242:         self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\n2243:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\n2244:         self.tracing_context.clear()\n2245:         self.input_source_to_var.clear()\n2246:         self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\n2247:         self.backward_state.clear()\n2248: \n2249:     def add_graph_finalizer(\n2250:         self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\n2251:     ) -> None:\n2252:         self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)\n2253: \n2254:     def example_value_from_input_node(self, node: torch.fx.Node) -> Any:\n2255:         \"\"\"Extract the non-fake example tensor\"\"\"\n2256:         if node.op == \"placeholder\":\n2257:             return node.meta[\"grapharg\"].example\n2258:         assert node.op == \"get_attr\"\n2259:         return self.nn_modules[node.target]  # type: ignore[index]\n2260: \n2261: \n2262: err_epilogue = (\n2263:     \"With the current config, we will graph break \"\n2264:     \"(and fall back to eager-mode PyTorch) on all ops \"\n2265:     \"that have do not have the 'pt2_compliant_tag'. \"\n2266:     \"Please see the following doc for how to mark this op as PT2 compliant \"\n2267:     \"https://pytorch.org/tutorials/advanced/custom_ops_landing_page.html\"\n2268: )\n2269: \n2270: \n2271: def check_pt2_compliant_op(\n2272:     output_graph: OutputGraph, kind: str, target: Any, args: Any, kwargs: Any\n2273: ) -> None:\n2274:     if kind != \"call_function\":\n2275:         return\n2276: \n2277:     def encountered_compliant_op(target: torch._ops.OpOverload) -> None:\n2278:         if target.namespace in {\"prim\", \"prims\", \"aten\"}:\n2279:             return\n2280:         output_graph.compliant_custom_ops.add(target)\n2281: ",

type: ignore[index]\\n2260: \\n2261: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2220-2260\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "2220: \n2221:         Returns the name of the newly installed global.\n2222:         \"\"\"\n2223:         # NB: unique_id is unique, even across torch.compile instances\n2224:         name = unique_id(prefix)\n2225:         self.install_global_unsafe(name, value)\n2226:         return name\n2227: \n2228:     def cleanup(self) -> None:\n2229:         # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\n2230:         # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\n2231:         self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\n2232:         self.nn_modules.clear()\n2233:         self.param_name_to_source = None\n2234: \n2235:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\n2236:             if \"grapharg\" in node.meta:\n2237:                 del node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n2238:         self.real_value_cache.clear()\n2239:         self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\n2240:         self.side_effects.clear()\n2241:         self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\n2242:         self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\n2243:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\n2244:         self.tracing_context.clear()\n2245:         self.input_source_to_var.clear()\n2246:         self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\n2247:         self.backward_state.clear()\n2248: \n2249:     def add_graph_finalizer(\n2250:         self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\n2251:     ) -> None:\n2252:         self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)\n2253: \n2254:     def example_value_from_input_node(self, node: torch.fx.Node) -> Any:\n2255:         \"\"\"Extract the non-fake example tensor\"\"\"\n2256:         if node.op == \"placeholder\":\n2257:             return node.meta[\"grapharg\"].example\n2258:         assert node.op == \"get_attr\"\n2259:         return self.nn_modules[node.target]  # type: ignore[index]\n2260: \n2261: ",

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "\n    def cleanup(self) -> None:\n        # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\n        # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\n        self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        self.nn_modules.clear()\n        self.param_name_to_source = None\n\n        for node in self.graph.nodes:\n            if \"grapharg\" in node.meta:\n                del node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n        self.real_value_cache.clear()\n        self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\n        self.side_effects.clear()\n        self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\n        self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\n        self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\n        self.tracing_context.clear()\n        self.input_source_to_var.clear()\n        self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\n        self.backward_state.clear()\n\n    def add_graph_finalizer(\n        self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\n    ) -> None:",
    "filePath": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py",
    "numLines": 25,
    "startLine": 2227,
    "totalLines": 3273
  },
  "type": "text"
}
106. Edit @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py",
  "new_string": "    def cleanup(self) -> None:\n        # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\n        # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\n        self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        self.nn_modules.clear()\n        self.param_name_to_source = None\n\n        for node in self.graph.nodes:\n            if \"grapharg\" in node.meta:\n                del node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n        self.real_value_cache.clear()\n        self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\n        self.side_effects.clear()\n        self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\n        self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\n        self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\n        self.tracing_context.clear()\n        self.input_source_to_var.clear()\n        self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\n        self.backward_state.clear()\n\n        # Break reference cycles in graph nodes to allow immediate garbage collection\n        # Nodes form a doubly-linked list where each node points to next/prev nodes\n        # and indirectly back to the graph, creating a cycle\n        for node in self.graph.nodes:\n            node._prev = None  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n            node._next = None  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n\n        # Break FakeTensorMode -> ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor -> FakeTensorMode cycle\n        self.shape_env.tracked_fakes.clear()",
  "old_string": "    def cleanup(self) -> None:\n        # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\n        # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\n        self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        self.nn_modules.clear()\n        self.param_name_to_source = None\n\n        for node in self.graph.nodes:\n            if \"grapharg\" in node.meta:\n                del node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n        self.real_value_cache.clear()\n        self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\n        self.side_effects.clear()\n        self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\n        self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\n        self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\n        self.tracing_context.clear()\n        self.input_source_to_var.clear()\n        self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\n        self.backward_state.clear()",
  "replace_all": false
}
Output
{
  "filePath": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py",
  "newString": "    def cleanup(self) -> None:\n        # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\n        # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\n        self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        self.nn_modules.clear()\n        self.param_name_to_source = None\n\n        for node in self.graph.nodes:\n            if \"grapharg\" in node.meta:\n                del node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n        self.real_value_cache.clear()\n        self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\n        self.side_effects.clear()\n        self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\n        self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\n        self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\n        self.tracing_context.clear()\n        self.input_source_to_var.clear()\n        self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\n        self.backward_state.clear()\n\n        # Break reference cycles in graph nodes to allow immediate garbage collection\n        # Nodes form a doubly-linked list where each node points to next/prev nodes\n        # and indirectly back to the graph, creating a cycle\n        for node in self.graph.nodes:\n            node._prev = None  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n            node._next = None  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n\n        # Break FakeTensorMode -> ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor -> FakeTensorMode cycle\n        self.shape_env.tracked_fakes.clear()",
  "oldString": "    def cleanup(self) -> None:\n        # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\n        # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\n        self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        self.nn_modules.clear()\n        self.param_name_to_source = None\n\n        for node in self.graph.nodes:\n            if \"grapharg\" in node.meta:\n                del node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n        self.real_value_cache.clear()\n        self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\n        self.side_effects.clear()\n        self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\n        self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\n        self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\n        self.tracing_context.clear()\n        self.input_source_to_var.clear()\n        self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\n        self.backward_state.clear()",
  "originalFile": "\"\"\"\nCore graph building functionality for PyTorch's Dynamo system. This module contains\nthe essential components for constructing and managing FX graphs during compilation:\n\n- OutputGraph: Manages the overall graph construction and compilation process. It owns\n  a SubgraphTracer and handles graph compilation, execution, and state management.\n  OutputGraph also manages features like graph deduplication, symbolic shape handling,\n  and tracking of side effects.\n\n- SubgraphTracer: Handles the actual FX graph construction by tracing Python code.\n  It supports advanced features like higher-order operators through nested tracers,\n  lifting of free variables, and handling of symbolic shapes.\n\nThe module supports key Dynamo features including:\n- Higher-order operators through nested SubgraphTracers\n- Graph deduplication for optimization\n- Symbolic shape handling and propagation\n- Side effect tracking and management\n- Guard insertion and management\n\"\"\"\n\nimport collections\nimport contextlib\nimport copy\nimport functools\nimport inspect\nimport itertools\nimport logging\nimport operator\nimport re\nimport sys\nimport traceback\nimport warnings\nimport weakref\nfrom collections.abc import Generator, Sequence\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field as dc_field\nfrom types import CodeType\nfrom typing import Any, Callable, cast, Optional, TYPE_CHECKING, Union\nfrom typing_extensions import ParamSpec, TypeVar\n\nimport sympy\n\nimport torch._guards\nimport torch._logging\nimport torch.distributed as dist\nimport torch.nn\nimport torch.utils._pytree as pytree\nfrom torch import fx, Tensor\nfrom torch._C._dynamo import guards\nfrom torch._dynamo.exc import ShortenTraceback, TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis\nfrom torch._guards import (\n    CompileContext,\n    CompileId,\n    GlobalContextCheckpointState,\n    Source,\n    tracing,\n    TracingContext,\n)\nfrom torch._subclasses.fake_tensor import FakeTensor\nfrom torch._utils_internal import signpost_event\nfrom torch.export.dynamic_shapes import _ConstraintTarget\nfrom torch.fx._lazy_graph_module import _make_graph_module  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\nfrom torch.fx.experimental._backward_state import BackwardState\nfrom torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import (\n    free_symbols,\n    guard_scalar,\n    is_symbolic,\n    ShapeEnv,\n    Specialization,\n)\nfrom torch.fx.node import Target\nfrom torch.fx.passes.runtime_assert import insert_deferred_runtime_asserts\nfrom torch.multiprocessing.reductions import StorageWeakRef\nfrom torch.utils._ordered_set import OrderedSet\nfrom torch.utils._python_dispatch import is_traceable_wrapper_subclass\n\nfrom . import config, exc, logging as torchdynamo_logging, variables\nfrom .backends.registry import CompiledFn, CompilerFn\nfrom .bytecode_transformation import (\n    create_call_function,\n    create_instruction,\n    create_load_const,\n    Instruction,\n    unique_id,\n)\nfrom .code_context import code_context\nfrom .codegen import PyCodegen\nfrom .current_scope_id import enter_new_scope\nfrom .device_interface import get_interface_for_device\nfrom .exc import (\n    BackendCompilerFailed,\n    exceptions_allowed_to_be_fallback,\n    SkipFrame,\n    unimplemented_v2,\n    unimplemented_v2_with_warning,\n)\nfrom .graph_deduplication import apply_graph_deduplication\nfrom .graph_region_tracker import GraphRegionTracker\nfrom .guards import GuardBuilder, install_guard\nfrom .mutation_guard import is_dynamic_nn_module\nfrom .side_effects import AttributeMutationExisting, SideEffects, ValueMutationExisting\nfrom .source import (\n    _get_source_debug_name,\n    AttrSource,\n    BackwardStateSource,\n    ConstantSource,\n    GetItemSource,\n    GlobalStateSource,\n    is_constant_source,\n    is_from_local_source,\n    LocalSource,\n    NumpyTensorSource,\n    ParamBufferSource,\n    ShapeEnvSource,\n    SyntheticLocalSource,\n    TensorProperty,\n    TensorPropertySource,\n)\nfrom .utils import (\n    _extract_tensor_dict,\n    checkpoint_params,\n    CleanupHook,\n    clone_inputs,\n    count_calls,\n    counters,\n    dynamo_timed,\n    get_instruction_source_311,\n    get_locals_to_steal,\n    get_static_address_type,\n    get_unique_name_wrt,\n    graph_break_reasons,\n    increment_op_count,\n    istype,\n    lazy_format_graph_code,\n    LazyString,\n    nn_module_proxy,\n    same,\n    set_example_value,\n)\nfrom .variables.base import VariableTracker\nfrom .variables.builder import (\n    BackwardStateGraphArg,\n    GraphArg,\n    TrackedFake,\n    wrap_fx_proxy,\n)\nfrom .variables.ctx_manager import ContextWrappingVariable\nfrom .variables.lists import BaseListVariable\nfrom .variables.misc import CellVariable, NullVariable\nfrom .variables.nn_module import NNModuleVariable\nfrom .variables.tensor import (\n    NumpyNdarrayVariable,\n    SymNodeVariable,\n    TensorVariable,\n    UnspecializedPythonVariable,\n)\nfrom .variables.torch_function import TensorWithTFOverrideVariable\nfrom .variables.user_defined import UserDefinedDictVariable\n\n\nif TYPE_CHECKING:\n    from torch._dynamo.package import CompilePackage\n    from torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert import InstructionTranslatorBase\n\nlog = logging.getLogger(__name__)\ngraph_tabular_log = torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \"graph\")\ngraph_code_log = torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \"graph_code\")\ngraph_sizes_log = torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \"graph_sizes\")\ntrace_call_log = torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \"trace_call\")\n\nRootGuardManager = guards.RootGuardManager\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass VariableTrackerCacheKey:\n    vt_id: int\n    # Two different source can point to the same object. However, Dynamo handles\n    # globals and local source differently when it comes to guards and possibly\n    # some other parts as well. So, cache also relies on the source.\n    source: Source\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass AliasingInfo:\n    has_aliasing: bool\n    msg: str\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass MutationInfo:\n    has_mutation: bool\n    msg: str\n\n\nclass VariableTrackerCache:\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self.cache: dict[VariableTrackerCacheKey, VariableTracker] = {}\n\n    def lookup(self, value: Any, source: Source) -> Optional[VariableTracker]:\n        key = VariableTrackerCacheKey(id(value), source)\n        if key not in self.cache:\n            return None\n        return self.cache[key]\n\n    def add(self, value: Any, source: Source, vt: VariableTracker) -> None:\n        key = VariableTrackerCacheKey(id(value), source)\n        self.cache[key] = vt\n\n    def clone(self) -> \"VariableTrackerCache\":\n        # Needed for copy and restore graph state\n        new_cache = VariableTrackerCache()\n        new_cache.cache.update(self.cache)\n        return new_cache\n\n    def clear(self) -> None:\n        self.cache.clear()\n\n\n@functools.cache\ndef _step_logger() -> Any:\n    return torchdynamo_logging.get_step_logger(log)\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass GraphCompileReason:\n    \"\"\"Stores why a given output graph was compiled; i.e. what caused the graph break.\"\"\"\n\n    reason: str\n    user_stack: list[traceback.FrameSummary]\n\n    # Indicates if this was a graph break reason due to graph break.\n    graph_break: bool = True\n\n    def __post_init__(self) -> None:\n        if self.graph_break:\n            graph_break_reasons.append(self)\n\n\ndef _get_gen_rand_values_fn(random_calls: Any) -> Callable[[], list[Any]]:\n    def _gen_rand_values() -> list[Any]:\n        return [fn(*args, **kwargs) for fn, args, kwargs in random_calls]\n\n    return _gen_rand_values\n\n\nclass FakeRootModule(torch.nn.Module):\n    \"\"\"Trick the constructor of fx.GraphModule\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, nn_modules: dict[str, torch.nn.Module]):\n        super().__init__()\n        for k, v in nn_modules.items():\n            setattr(self, k, v)\n\n    def __repr__(self) -> str:\n        return \"FakeRootModule(...)\"\n\n    def add_nn_modules(self, nn_modules: dict[str, torch.nn.Module]) -> None:\n        for k, v in nn_modules.items():\n            setattr(self, k, v)\n\n\nclass WrapperBackend:\n    def __init__(self, backend: CompilerFn) -> None:\n        self.backend: CompilerFn = backend\n\n    def __call__(\n        self, gm: torch.fx.GraphModule, example_inputs: list[torch.Tensor]\n    ) -> CompiledFn:\n        self.restore = checkpoint_params(gm)\n        self.gm = gm\n        copy_gm = copy.deepcopy(self.gm)\n        self.candidate = self.backend(copy_gm, example_inputs)\n\n        if self.candidate is None or self.candidate is self.gm.forward:\n            return self.gm.forward\n\n        if not config.verify_correctness:\n            return self.candidate\n\n        # if verify_correctness=True\n        try:\n            correct = self.gm.forward(*clone_inputs(example_inputs))\n            result = self.candidate(*clone_inputs(example_inputs))\n\n            # TODO: replace `same` function with the one in testing\n            if same(correct, result):\n                return self.candidate\n\n            raise RuntimeError(f\"incorrect results of backend {self}\")\n\n        except Exception:\n            log.exception(\"error in verify_correctness\")\n            raise\n        finally:\n            self.restore()\n\n\nScope = dict[str, object]\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass OutputGraphGuardsState:\n    \"\"\"\n    A base class containing fields that are considered \"persistent\" when we\n    want to save all the important state for reconstrucing guards in a different\n    process. Normally we don't need to add states here, but we may have to when\n    the information is needed to serialize the guards, so the fields here are\n    supposed to be serializable as a requirement.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    local_scope: Scope\n    global_scope: Scope\n    # This records the initial torch function mode stack for guarding\n    torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode]\n    guard_on_key_order: set[Source]\n    # Map from graph input's `Source` to sizes / strides metadata\n    input_source_to_sizes_strides: dict[Source, dict[str, Any]]\n    dual_level: int\n    functorch_layers: list[torch._functorch.pyfunctorch.FuncTorchInterpreter]\n    current_device: Optional[torch.device]\n    global_state_guard: torch._C._dynamo.guards.GlobalStateGuard\n    _guards: torch._guards.GuardsSet\n    _aotautograd_guards: list[torch._guards.GuardEnvExpr]\n\n    export: bool = False\n    export_constraints: bool = False\n    name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals: Optional[str] = None\n\n    @property\n    def shape_env(self) -> ShapeEnv:\n        raise AssertionError(f\"shape_env shouldn't be accessed from {type(self)}\")\n\n    @property\n    def guards(self) -> torch._guards.GuardsSet:\n        return self._guards\n\n    @property\n    def aotautograd_guards(self) -> list[torch._guards.GuardEnvExpr]:\n        return self._aotautograd_guards\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass StackLocalsMetadata:\n    \"\"\"\n    Stores metadata for a frame's stack and locals for the purposes of building resume functions\n    \"\"\"\n\n    stack_null_idxes: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    locals_null_keys: list[str] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    stack_ctx_args: list[tuple[int, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    stack_ctx_idxes_orig: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    locals_ctx_args: list[tuple[str, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n\n\ndef get_builtins_dict(global_scope: Scope) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    # f_globals[\"__builtins__\"] can be a dict or a module. This is an\n    # implementation detail -\n    # https://docs.python.org/3/library/builtins.html.\n\n    # This makes guarding on any builtin messy because the guard check_fn\n    # has to check if the __builtins__ is a module or dict, and then access\n    # by either using getattr or getitem respectively.\n\n    # To solve this problem, we insert a new entry in f_globals which points\n    # to the builtins __dict__ and then we guard any builtin on this dict.\n    # To avoid any collision with the pre-existing keys, we use the\n    # install_global to give us a unique dict key.\n\n    f_builtins = global_scope[\"__builtins__\"]\n    if not isinstance(f_builtins, dict):\n        f_builtins = f_builtins.__dict__\n    return f_builtins\n\n\nclass OutputGraph(OutputGraphGuardsState):\n    \"\"\"\n    Wrapper class to hold outputs of InstructionTranslator.  Mainly the\n    generated fx.Graph.\n\n    OutputGraph is 1:1 with a frame being processed. Each frame is associated\n    with some root InstructionTranslator. When user code calls a function,\n    we construct a InliningInstructionTranslator that continues to write into\n    the root InstructionTranslator's OutputGraph.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    side_effects: SideEffects\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        code_options: dict[str, Any],\n        compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn],\n        root_tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\",\n        export: bool,\n        export_constraints: Sequence[_ConstraintTarget],\n        frame_state: Any,\n        local_scope: Scope,\n        global_scope: Scope,\n        f_code: CodeType,\n        torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode],\n        package: Optional[\"CompilePackage\"],\n    ) -> None:\n        super().__init__(\n            local_scope,\n            global_scope,\n            torch_function_mode_stack,\n            guard_on_key_order=set(),\n            input_source_to_sizes_strides={},\n            dual_level=torch.autograd.forward_ad._current_level,\n            functorch_layers=torch._functorch.pyfunctorch.retrieve_all_functorch_interpreters(),\n            current_device=torch.utils._device.CURRENT_DEVICE,\n            # initial_global_state is only None during NopTest.\n            global_state_guard=torch._dynamo.convert_frame.initial_global_state\n            or torch._C._dynamo.guards.GlobalStateGuard(),\n            # These are set by @property instead, just initialize them as blank\n            _guards=torch._guards.GuardsSet(),\n            _aotautograd_guards=[],\n        )\n        self.tracers = [SubgraphTracer(self, is_export=export)]\n        # Map from graph input's `Source` to its `VariableTracker` to\n        # de-duplicate graph inputs by source and reuse the tracker\n        self.input_source_to_var: dict[Source, VariableTracker] = {}\n        self.export = export\n        self.export_constraints = export_constraints  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        self.frame_state = frame_state\n        self.cleanup_hooks: list[Callable[[], Any]] = []\n        # compile_id is an id number for the current torch.compile\n        self.compile_id: int = next(_compile_id_counter)\n        # Set of globals installed via install_global* APIs\n        self.installed_globals: set[str] = set()\n\n        # TODO: maybe should just pass the entire f_code in here?  Not\n        # sure...\n        self.co_fields = {\n            \"co_name\": f_code.co_name,\n            \"co_filename\": f_code.co_filename,\n            \"co_firstlineno\": f_code.co_firstlineno,\n        }\n\n        self.region_tracker = GraphRegionTracker()\n\n        # tracked_fakes says where any tensor that was wrapped to fake came\n        # from.  It is similar to GraphArg, in that all GraphArgs will get\n        # will get added to TrackedFakes, but TrackedFakes also contains\n        # GraphArgs that got pruned, and things like Tensor attributes which\n        # aren't explicit graph inputs.  Used by shape guard\n        self.tracked_fakes: list[TrackedFake] = []\n\n        shape_env = ShapeEnv(\n            # Reference Cycle!\n            # Share a reference to the list of TrackedFake.\n            #\n            # ShapeEnv needs this in order to be able to reproduce the call\n            # to produce_guards at an arbitrary time point. That is because\n            # TrackedFake instances may have its metadata changed throughout\n            # the program execution.\n            tracked_fakes=self.tracked_fakes,\n            allow_scalar_outputs=config.capture_scalar_outputs,\n            allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=config.capture_dynamic_output_shape_ops,\n            prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards=config.prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards,\n            allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts=config.allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts,\n            co_fields=self.co_fields,\n        )\n\n        # In export mode, we force the shape_env to strictly disallow any constraining\n        # of the user marked dynamic dims\n        import torch._functorch.config as _config\n\n        with _config.patch(fake_tensor_allow_unsafe_data_ptr_access=False):\n            fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(\n                shape_env=shape_env,\n                # TODO (tmanlaibaatar) Remove this once we always lift params and buffers\n                allow_non_fake_inputs=True if self.export else False,\n                export=self.export,\n            )\n        self.tracing_context: TracingContext = TracingContext(fake_mode)\n        self.tracing_context.traced_code.append(f_code)\n        self.dynamo_compile_id: Optional[CompileId] = (\n            CompileContext.current_compile_id()\n        )\n        self.init_ambient_guards()\n\n        # Map each tensor id to a list of sources. This is necessary because\n        # tensor ids cannot be recovered from tracked fakes (in general).\n        # We use this map to interpret (i.e., check for violations of) constraints,\n        # specifically equality constraints, which have shared tensor ids in them.\n        # This map should also be generally useful, e.g., for (de)serialization.\n        self.tracked_fakes_id_to_source: dict[int, list[Source]] = (\n            collections.defaultdict(list)\n        )\n        # Stores the full fqn of a param or buffer to the relevant source.\n        self.param_name_to_source: Optional[dict[str, Source]] = {}\n        self.side_effects = SideEffects(self)\n        # Cached variable trackers. This makes symbolic analysis of LOAD_GLOBAL\n        # and LOAD_ATTR for same python objects free.\n        self.variable_tracker_cache = VariableTrackerCache()\n        self.unique_var_id = itertools.count()\n        self.code_options: dict[str, Any] = dict(code_options)\n        self.output_instructions: list[Instruction] = []\n        # used to track nodes that are added between calls of copy_graphstate\n        # and restore_graphstate\n        self.timestamp = 0\n\n        # A list of register_finalizer_fns to apply to the output graph module\n        self.register_finalizer_fns: list[Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]] = []\n\n        # Not checkpointed\n        self.compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn] = compiler_fn\n        self.root_tx = root_tx\n\n        self.package = package\n        # Given a source, what are the user stacks of all locations that\n        # accessed it?\n        #\n        # For efficiency, we only populate this:\n        #   - During export, and\n        #   - If the source could potentially lead to a spurious export input\n        #\n        # Feel free to populate this more frequently if other use-cases arise,\n        # but be aware that we have to generate full stacks for each\n        # recording!\n        self.source_to_user_stacks: dict[Source, list[traceback.StackSummary]] = {}\n\n        self._current_tx: list[InstructionTranslatorBase] = []\n        self.cleanups: list[CleanupHook] = []\n        self.should_exit = False\n        self.unspec_variable_map: dict[str, UnspecializedPythonVariable] = {}\n\n        # This returns false if TF Overall (both mode and subclass) is disabled OR that TF Mode stack is empty\n        self.torch_function_mode_enabled = torch._C._is_torch_function_mode_enabled()\n\n        # Tracks if the output graph has a user defined allowed function in the\n        # graph. This is used later to determine if we should fallback to eager\n        # for certain exceptions. THe idea is that if the user has applied\n        # allow_in_graph, they would like to see the error instead of falling\n        # back for backend errors.\n        self.has_user_defined_allowed_in_graph = False\n\n        # Tracks a list of called ops that were not tagged with \"pt2_compliant_tag\".\n        # This information is useful for logging.\n        self.non_compliant_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\n\n        # Tracks a list of called custom ops that were tagged with \"pt2_compliant_tag\".\n        # This information is useful for logging.\n        self.compliant_custom_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\n\n        # We save the global torch state here to be restored in case of graph\n        # breaks. The relevant issue is seen here\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/100570#issuecomment-1543427086\n        # where inlining of a function changes the global state (because of the\n        # presence of torch.no_grad) and there is a graph break.\n        self.save_global_state()\n\n        # Tracks the original FQNs of the constant tensors from the original graph,\n        # i.e. buffers and parameters.\n        self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn: dict[str, str] = {}\n\n        # All calls to random() are replaced with a single call to __gen_rand_values\n        # functions that returns a tuple of random values for each original call.\n        # random_calls tracks calls to random() and random_values_var stores the name of\n        # the variable that stores __gen_rand_values results.\n        self.random_calls: list[\n            tuple[Callable[..., object], tuple[object, ...], dict[str, object]]\n        ] = []\n        self.random_values_var: Any = None\n\n        # Bytecode to insert right before we call the graph\n        self.pregraph_bytecode: list[Instruction] = []\n\n        # Use to pass values to backward hooks when using compiled autograd\n        self.backward_state: dict[str, VariableTracker] = {}\n        self.backward_state_proxy: Optional[torch.fx.Proxy] = None\n        self.backward_state_var: Optional[str] = None\n\n        self.name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals: str = (\n            self.install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals()\n        )\n\n        self.compiler_trace_stack = contextlib.ExitStack()\n\n        # These are the ambient, currently-global saved_tensor_hooks stashed in autograd,\n        # that are set for the entire duration of the compiled region.\n        # This is an invariant today because we graph break on the saved_tensor_hook\n        # context manager inside a compiled region\n        self.saved_tensors_hooks_subgraph_names: Optional[list[str]] = (\n            self.maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs()\n        )\n\n    def mark_bytecode_tracing_start(self) -> None:\n        self.compiler_trace_stack.enter_context(\n            dynamo_timed(\n                \"bytecode_tracing\",\n                log_pt2_compile_event=True,\n            )\n        )\n\n    def mark_bytecode_tracing_stop(self) -> None:\n        self.compiler_trace_stack.close()\n\n    def install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals(self) -> str:\n        f_builtins = get_builtins_dict(self.global_scope)\n        return self.install_global(\"__builtins_dict__\", f_builtins)\n\n    def add_backward_state_hook(\n        self, hook: VariableTracker, prefix: str = \"hook\"\n    ) -> tuple[str, torch.fx.Proxy]:\n        name = f\"{prefix}{len(self.backward_state)}\"\n        assert name not in self.backward_state\n        self.backward_state[name] = hook\n        return name, self.get_backward_state_proxy()\n\n    def get_backward_state_proxy(self) -> torch.fx.Proxy:\n        if self.backward_state_proxy is None:\n            if self.export:\n                unimplemented_v2(\n                    gb_type=\"backward_state does not support export\",\n                    context=\"\",\n                    explanation=\"Compiled autograd doesn't work with `torch.export`.\",\n                    hints=[],\n                )\n            example_value = BackwardState()\n            self.backward_state_proxy = self.root_tracer.create_graph_input(\n                \"dynamo_backward_state\",\n                type(example_value),\n                example_value,\n                source=BackwardStateSource(),\n            )\n            self.backward_state_proxy.node.meta[\"grapharg\"] = BackwardStateGraphArg()\n            self.backward_state_var = self.new_var()\n        return self.backward_state_proxy\n\n    # This gets its own helper function so guards DEBUG logs are more informative\n    def init_ambient_guards(self) -> None:\n        # Register a SHAPE_ENV guard to make sure we setup shape guards\n        #
